entry.title: Empowering Public Sector Users of SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
For many governments around the world, driving efficiency and leveraging technological advances to increase workforce productivity are high on their list of priorities. At SAP, we harness the right AI tools working within the context of your data, to offer you real-world results that make a difference to your business. This is what we refer to as SAP Business AI and SAP Joule is your business AI assistant, grounded in SAP data. 

After the 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, government agencies in Australia, Canada, Germany, The Netherlands, United Kingdom and the United States of America, can look forward to AI innovations that SAP is planning to introduce next in Public Sector Management (PSM). The PSM scope bundle consists of Grants Management, Budget Management and Funds Accounting. Specifically, SAP will be adding budget and grant management context to SAP Joule. With context awareness, SAP Joule is designed to streamline and improve the user experience, allowing users to efficiently navigate their financial systems and retrieve information to make better strategic decisions. New users typically require extensive training, SAP Joule can quickly provide definitions, summaries, and how-to guidance, saving time and reducing enquiries to IT departments.

The SAP Road Map Explorer provides information on what and when the innovations are planned, and the SAP Process Navigator provides information on where these innovations will be available. Let us have a look at what these planned changes in PSM entail.

The User Experience - Reimagined

Interactions with SAP Joule are carried out using natural language, just like how you communicate with your friendly colleagues each day! No learning curve to overcome before you get the answers you need. 

Use SAP Joule to get quick answers to your questions and save time searching through lengthy documentation. No question is too basic for Joule and Joule can even make sense of your shorthand!
  

Efficient Navigation and Task Execution

Today, SAP supports many customers across the globe who run highly complex business processes. Users often utilize different functions and screens to perform multiple transactions. SAP Joule can help users quickly and efficiently navigate to relevant applications to complete their tasks. In our Early Adopter program, participants have reported time savings of up to 90% when performing navigational and transactional tasks. With Joule’s help, a task that used to require 5 minutes was reduced to 30 seconds. This can potentially lead to significant cost savings for public sector organizations. Let’s estimate the potential savings an agency with 10,000 employees could realize: 

 

Joule can help reduce the time to perform routine tasks by quickly bringing you to the relevant application screen. Instead of navigating through menu options, simply enter a short query such as “Show Funds”. You will be provided with options to access the relevant screen directly or to read related documentation. 

Click on the “Open” button and SAP Joule directly brings you to the “Manage Funds” screen where you can search and display information about various funds. You can create or delete funds, or access individual funds to make changes.

A new user can quickly learn about the “Manage Funds” application by clicking on the SAP Help link provided by Joule.

Here’s another example of how you can easily navigate the application and perform your tasks more quickly with the help of SAP Joule:

To make changes to a grant, simply say what you want to do and SAP Joule will guide you.

When you click on the “Open” button to manage grants, SAP Joule brings you directly to the screen where you can view or amend details of the grant.

A user who is unfamiliar with Grants Management can learn more by clicking on the SAP Help link provided by Joule. Joule ensures that the most relevant content is presented to you right from the start, saving you time scrolling through multiple pages of less important information.


We are just starting our journey with SAP Joule, and our customers can expect more AI innovations to be added across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Information on these plans can be found on the SAP Road Map Explorer. To find out more about AI and its potential to serve your needs in the public sector, please read our whitepaper, “AI in the Public Sector”.

entry.title: Partner Demo Environment - Phase 10 AI Feature Release
entry.content:
We are excited to share with you the latest updates to our PDE (Shared Demo Environment) Phase 10 AI Release.

This release introduces a range of cutting-edge AI features across various SAP solutions, designed to enhance productivity, streamline processes, and drive better business outcomes. 

In this release, Gen-AI and Joule features are available across the suite, including Customer Experience (2), #Ariba(2), #SuccessFactors#(1), and BTP(1)

Sales & Service Cloud V2

SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 now features cutting-edge Generative AI capabilities designed to enhance sales and service processes. These innovative features leverage AI to streamline workflows, improve decision-making, and enhance customer interactions.

The following table provides an overview of the key Generative AI features available in this solution:

FeatureDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
Case SummaryGenerative AI summarizes text in email interactions for cases, providing a quick overview of customer communications.- Improved efficiency in case handling- Faster response times due to quick comprehension of case details- Enhanced customer service quality through better understanding of issues
Email DrafterUses Generative AI to draft email replies or interactions for cases and opportunities, streamlining communication processes.- Time savings in composing emails- Consistency in messaging across the organization- Improved quality and professionalism of communications
Account SynopsisProvides comprehensive information related to an account, helping sales and service professionals. It covers:
• Overall Information
• Business Overview
• Current Challenges
• Competitive Landscape
• Culture and Background
• Business Strategies
• Typical Customers		- Holistic view of customer accounts- Better prepared sales and service teams- More informed decision-making in customer interactions
Lead BoosterOffers customized information about a lead to help sales representatives win deals. It includes:
• About the Company
• Challenges
• Alignment with Customer Goals
• Customer Pain Points
• Benefits
• Customization and Scalability
• Competitive Advantage Maximization		- Helps sales representatives win the deal (stated in document)- Accelerates lead-to-opportunity conversion- Enables more personalized and effective sales strategies

Demo Asset 19197

Customer Experience 

SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement

Leverages advanced AI capabilities to optimize various aspects of digital marketing. The following features enhance marketer efficiency and campaign effectiveness through AI-powered tools.

FeatureDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
AI Subject Line Generator- Generates or improves subject lines with AI-driven suggestions- Includes personalization and emojis- Enables A/B testing of AI-generated and traditional subject lines- Saves time and effort in creating subject lines- Enhances creativity with new subject-line ideas- Enables data-driven decisions through A/B testing
AI Content Block Generator- Creates personalized content blocks based on natural language prompts- Optimizes content while ensuring brand tone of voice- Assists in product search for campaign insertion- Creates personalized content across customer engagement channels- Accelerates campaign content creation- Maintains brand consistency- Drives greater value from product catalog data- Improves content relevance and personalization
AI Product Finder (Pilot)- Enables intuitive search of a brand's product catalog- Quickly locates suitable products for campaigns- Out-of-the-box solution for email marketing campaigns- Improves efficiency in product selection for campaigns- Scales creation of curated product content- Enhances message relevancy and engagement
AI Segment Generator (Pilot)- Generates human-readable descriptions of existing segments- Can create new segments from natural language prompts- Distills complex criteria, logic, and operators into comprehensible descriptions- Improves understanding of customer segments- Enhances searchability of segments- Simplifies creation of new, targeted segments
  • The Subject Line Generator is set for General Availability in July 2024.
  • The AI Product Finder and AI Segment Generator are currently in the pilot phase.
  • These features are part of SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement, integrated with SAP Business AI.
  • The demo scenario uses specific email templates and segments. It's recommended not to alter these for demonstration purposes.

Demo Asset 19326

SAP Emarsys Loyalty 

 

SAP Business Technology Platform

This summary highlights the AI features provided by SAP Build Code for enhancing customer loyalty programs and application development. These features leverage generative AI to streamline the development process and improve customer engagement.

 

Feature NameDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
AI-powered Code Generation with Joule Copilot- Generates app logic from natural language descriptions- Optimized for Java and JavaScript application development- Provides a turn-key environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management- Increases developer productivity- Enables rapid application development- Simplifies complex coding tasks- Reduces time-to-market for new applications
Data Entity Generation- Creates data models and service entities using natural language prompts- Automatically generates sample data for testing and previewing- Accelerates database design process- Ensures consistency in data structure- Reduces errors in data modeling
Backend Logic Generation- Generates backend logic for operations like purchases, reward points calculation, and redemptions- Creates complex business rules from simple natural language descriptions- Streamlines implementation of business logic- Ensures consistency in applying business rules. Reduces development time for core functionalities
Fiori UI Application Design- Generates user interfaces for entities using AI-assisted templates- Creates responsive applications suitable for various devices

- Accelerates UI development process- Ensures consistency in user experience across applications

- Improves overall application usability and adoption

Fusion Development Approach- Combines professional developers' work (using Build Code) with citizen developers' contributions (using Build Apps)- Enables seamless integration between backend services and frontend applications- Facilitates collaboration between IT and business users- Speeds up overall application development lifecycle- -Allows for more flexible and agile development processes
  • The solution is based on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and benefits from its security features for authentication, authorization, and data protection.
  • SAP Build Code is optimized for SAP development and provides prebuilt integrations, APIs, and business services.
  • The solution enables quick extension of enterprise applications such as SAP S/4HANA.
  • Sample data generated by the AI is intended for testing and demo purposes only and should not be deployed to production.
  • The AI-generated code may vary for the same prompts, but as long as it achieves the desired functionality, it can be used.

Demo Asset 19247

SAP Build Code Generation Interactive Value Journey

SAP Build Code Joule Artificial Intelligence 

 

SAP Ariba Category Management

SAP Ariba Category Management offers a comprehensive suite of tools to enhance category planning, strategy execution, and procurement processes.

This summary covers both AI-powered features and core functionalities described in the attached documents.

Feature NameDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
Generative AI-Enhanced Content Recommendations- Available for Category Segmentation, Market Dynamics, and Cost Structure tools- Prefills content in Notes, Summary, and Additional Resources sections based on AI-retrieved content- Provides additional information articles based on category, organization, region, and other input parameters- Accelerates category strategy development- Provides contextual insights for better decision-making- Reduces time spent on manual research and data gathering- Enhances the quality and depth of category analysis
BEROE Integration- Integrates market intelligence reports from BEROE into category planning- Provides news feed in Strategy Execution dashboard with BEROE data- Allows users to add data from BEROE provider in Market Dynamics tool- Enhances market analysis with external expert insights- Keeps category managers informed of market trends and news- Improves strategic decision-making with up-to-date market intelligence- Reduces time spent on manual market research
Category Segmentation- Uses Kraljic matrix to segment categories based on business impact and supply market complexity- Visualizes subcategories within the main category- Supports AI-generated content for segmentation analysis- Helps in strategic categorization of spend- Offers insights for tailored category strategies- Facilitates better risk and opportunity management
Market Dynamics Analysis- Utilizes Porter's Five Forces model- Integrates with BEROE for market intelligence data- Supports AI-generated content- Provides comprehensive view of market forces- Enhances understanding of competitive landscape- Aids in developing more informed sourcing strategies
Cost Structure Analysis- Breaks down category costs into components- Employs AI to generate cost structure content- Offers insights into cost drivers- Improves understanding of cost drivers- Aids in identifying cost reduction opportunities- Facilitates more effective negotiations with suppliers
Strategy and Implementation Planning- Allows setting of category goals, value levers, and initiatives- Links initiatives to specific goals- Estimates benefits and investments for initiatives- Aligns category strategy with organizational objectives- Provides clear roadmap for strategy execution- Facilitates tracking of strategy implementation and results
Integration with SAP Ariba Sourcing- Links category initiatives to Guided Sourcing projects- Allows tracking of sourcing project progress within Category Management- Ensures seamless execution of category strategies- Provides visibility into sourcing activities- Facilitates measurement of strategy effectiveness
Monitor Strategy Execution- Displays progress on category goals- Shows status of ongoing initiatives- Integrates with planning calendar, contracts, and sourcing events- Enables real-time tracking of strategy execution- Facilitates proactive management of category performance- Provides holistic view of category-related activities
Category Profile Management- Stores category-specific information (laws, regulations, team members, stakeholders)- Supports document uploads for relevant policies and regulations- Centralizes critical category information- Ensures compliance with relevant laws and policies- Facilitates stakeholder management
  • Category taxonomy management is an administrative function, demonstrated separately through an InVision demo link.
  • The solution integrates with SAP Analytics Cloud for spend analysis visualizations.

Demo Asset 19218

Demo Asset 19219

Category Management Interactive Value Journey

SAP Ariba solution integration for SAP Business Suite 

 

SAP SuccessFactors 

SAP SuccessFactors has integrated advanced AI features to enhance the recruitment process and overall employee experience. These features leverage artificial intelligence to streamline HR operations, improve job description creation, and facilitate better feedback processes.

The following summary highlights the key AI feature: AI-enhanced job descriptions.

Feature NameDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
AI-Enhanced Job Descriptions• Uses generative AI to create compelling job descriptions
• Leverages data from job requisitions and existing job profiles
• Allows side-by-side comparison of original and AI-generated versions
• Utilizes skills and competencies data from the Talent Intelligence Hub
• Provides user-friendly configuration options within SAP SuccessFactors admin settings		• Streamlines the recruitment process
• Attracts a diverse range of candidates
• Enhances alignment between job requirements and candidate qualifications
• Saves time for recruiters to craft job descriptions
• Improves consistency and quality of job postings across the organization

Demo Asset 19336

SuccessFactors AI Interactive Value Journey

#Generative AI Artificial Intelligence

entry.title: Generative AI with SAP – Part 2: Components of SAP Business AI
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Generative AI with SAP, which is focused on understanding how SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across its portfolio.

In this series we are learning:

  • How SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across it’s portfolio.
  • What SAP Business AI is and how SAP partners and customers can use it?
  • What is SAP AI Core? What is SAP AI launchpad? What is Joule? etc.
  • What role SAP BTP plays in SAP's AI strategy? ... and many more.

 

To make it easy to understand, the blog series is divided in small parts. Each blog requires maximum 10–15 minutes to learn.

 

  • Part 1 – Introduction to SAP Business AI 
  • Part 2 – Components of SAP Business AI [Current blog]
  • Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot
  • Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP 
  • Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core
  • Part 6 – Introduction to SAP AI launchpad
  • Part 7 – Get Started with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad
  • part 8 - Hello World using SAP AI Core
  • Part 9 – Overview of Generative AI Hub
  • Part 10 – SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
  • Part 11 - RAG, a real-life use case using SAP AI Core, Generative AI Hub and HANA Vector Engine

 

This is the 2nd blog in the series, where will learn what are major components of SAP Business AI.

Note: I will publish the subsequent  blogs soon.

 

Components of SAP Business AI

Major components of SAP Business AI are:

  • Joule
  • Embedded AI Applications
  • AI Foundation

Below image gives a high level overview of each component.

 

 

Now, let’s take a close look into these components.

 

Joule

Joule is:

  • An advanced, generative AI copilot
  • embedded throughout SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio
  • To deliver proactive and contextualized insights from across the breadth and depth of SAP’s applications and third-party sources

Joule is designed to helps people get work done faster and drive better business outcomes in a secure, compliant way.

Below summarizes major points about Joule.

 

For more information on Joule, refer to Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

 

Embedded AI Capabilities

Under the umbrella of SAP Business AI, SAP is embedding the capabilities of AI across entire SAP solutions. These AI-powered capabilities include matching, intelligent business document processing, personalized recommendations, natural language processing, forecasting and predictions, and many more.

SAP is offering these embedded AI capabilities across all business areas including finance, supply chain, procurement, human resources, sales, marketing, and IT.

Here are some examples:

In Finance, SAP Cash Application allow customers to automate invoice matching. It automates and simplify the order-to-cash process in accounts receivable accounting.

In Supply Chain, SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain allow customers to predict demand reliably with AI-powered demand forecasting.

In Human Resources, Personalized Recommendation, one of the SAP AI Business Services embedded in SAP SuccessFactors enable customers to improve the employee experience at scale.

You may refer to this page to know more about how customers can benefit from AI built into the core business processes — connecting finance, supply chain, procurement, sales, marketing, human resources, and IT as shown in image below.

 

AI Foundation on SAP BTP

AI foundation on SAP BTP is a set of ready-to-use AI services and tools, which:

  • enable customers and partners
  • to create AI-powered extensions and applications
  • in a reliable, secure, and scalable way

 

Below image gives a high-level picture of AI foundation.

 

 Note: For more information on AI foundation, refer to Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP

 

SAP’s Partnership with AI Vendors to Provide an Abstraction for SAP Customers

SAP has partnered with leading general-purpose AI vendors and large language model (LLM) providers to ensure that SAP customers are able to reap the benefit of AI innovations without going into individual contract with 3rd party vendors. SAP has made several strategic partnerships with Google, IBM, Nvidia, Microsoft and others.

For example:

SAP is leveraging Microsoft's Azure OpenAI Service to provide you access to powerful LLMs like GPT-3 without any 3rd party contract. From Generative AI Hub, you can access these large language models from multiple vendors easily.

SAP has partnered with NVIDIA to leverage its AI hardware and software solutions, for example NVIDIA V100 Tensor Core GPU can be used as one of the underlying infrastructures for SAP AI Core.  

 

Three Core Principles of SAP Business AI

SAP Business AI is built with three core principles: relevant, reliable, and responsible.

Relevant

In general, "relevant" means that AI systems should provide responses which are perfectly aligned with the user's current needs and context.

For example, if you ask an AI virtual assistant – “Find me a good restaurant to go now”, it should give the list of restaurants best on your specific food preferences and the current time. If it’s a dinner time, and the virtual assistant suggest a breakfast café OR if you are a vegetarian and it suggests a non-vegetarian restaurant, then it’s not Relevant for you.

Similarly, SAP Business AI understand the business context to become more relevant for customers and end users.

SAP Business AI is relevant from day one since it is built in across all SAP applications that customers already use to run their most critical business processes. These embedded AI capabilities are designed keeping business context in mind.

 

Reliable

In general, "reliable" refers to the consistency, accuracy, and dependability of the AI system in performing its tasks and producing the desired outcomes.

For example, a predictive AI model is considered reliable only if consistently predicts outcomes accurately, whether it's for stock prices, weather forecasting, or patient diagnoses. Or a chatbot is considered reliable only if understands user queries correctly and provides accurate and helpful responses consistently.

Reliability is one of the main core principles of SAP Business AI.

The services and solutions under SAP Business AI are built on rigorous product standards, and enterprise-readiness that customers expect from SAP. These AI applications are trained on industry and business process data, making it more reliable for customers.

Customers can trust the SAP Business AI to perform its intended function accurately, consistently, and dependably.

 

Responsible

In general, AI system being "responsible" refers to ensuring that AI systems are developed, deployed, and used in a manner that aligns with ethical principles, respects human rights, promotes fairness, and mitigates potential risks or harms.

SAP is deeply committed to AI ethics in line with our core values. SAP Business AI is designed for people to get their best work done, valuing human oversight and agency.

SAP system contains business critical and confidential data. SAP customers trust SAP with their mission-critical business processes and most sensitive data. Therefore, SAP AI applications and services are built using responsible AI. SAP uses a robust ethical framework that guides the development and operations of AI.

 

What’s Next?

Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

 

Disclaimer – All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

entry.title: New pilots to empower customers to leverage AI and SAP HANA Cloud capabilities to innovate faster
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence or AI is disrupting business everywhere! Organizations are looking at ways to innovate and update their business processes to make best use of AI to make savings and add improved functionality to their customers. As we come to terms with AI’s vast potential, SAP has adjusted its strategy with the goal of becoming not only the leading enterprise application provider but also the number one business AI company. SAP provides opportunities to use AI to unlock new levels of efficiency and innovation in their customer’s businesses.

To support this strategy, in SAP Enterprise Support we are providing two upcoming pilots of Expert Guided Implementation services through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council program designed to empower customers with the knowledge and skills to leverage SAP HANA Cloud’s advanced capabilities and AI to improve efficiency and innovation in organizations.

Expert Guided Implementation (EGI) services are multi-day remote delivered sessions, delivered through by SAP Enterprise Support Academy and typically delivered over 3-5 working days. In short, this is a virtual classroom style session, where an SAP expert explains step-by-step the configuration of a set objective using training materials and step-by-step demonstration.

In our first pilot EGI service, we delve into Multiple Model Programming using the SAP HANA Vector Engine and explore how Generative AI, specifically Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), can transform customer’s data management and AI strategies. The service is designed to provide a comprehensive understanding of SAP HANA Cloud’s advanced features and how they can be leveraged to support cutting-edge AI applications. By the end of the service, customers will be equipped with skills to implement multiple model programming, utilize the Vector Engine, and enhance your AI models with Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). Plus, insights into effective data tiering strategies to optimize data management will be provided.

In our second pilot EGI service, we equip customers with the skills and knowledge to harness the power of the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model in SAP HANA Cloud. The service will introduce you to SAP Build Code and Joule, SAP’s generative AI assistant, to streamline application development processes. The service provides with a comprehensive understanding of the SAP CAP model and how to effectively use SAP Build Code and Joule to accelerate your application development. By the end of the service, customers will have the skills to develop robust CAP applications, utilize SAP HANA Cloud for data management, and leverage generative AI to enhance your coding efficiency.

Please join us for this exciting journey into the world of Generative AI, Joule and SAP HANA Cloud and unlock new possibilities for your business! For further queries including how to join these pilots, reach out to the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council directly at mailto:esac@sap.com.

Thanks and Regards,

Robert Kelly

BTP Lead – SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Joule and SAP Business AI Now available for RISE with SAP Customers
entry.content:
SAP’s vision for Business AI is to bring powerful Generative AI capabilities across all our products and services through SAP BTP to benefit the customers in achieving measurable business outcomes. As you are already aware, SAP introduced Joule in Sep 2023, a copilot that truly understands your business revolutionizing the interactions with SAP systems making every touchpoint count and every task simpler in a secure and compliant way. Joule will be embedded across SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio, delivering proactive and contextualized insights from SAP solutions and third-party sources. Joule is everywhere, was one of the most discussed topics at SAPPHIRE 2024, and needs no introduction, find out everything you need to know about Joule on this blog.

We are excited to announce Joule is now available for RISE with SAP customers with significant enhancements to RISE with SAP commercial packages focused on accelerating digital transformation and bringing the power of AI to all our customers. Joule, our AI copilot, will now be available for all existing and new RISE with SAP customers, natively integrating into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Since launching RISE with SAP in Jan 2021, our mission to transform customers into a cloud operating model had a strong momentum in RISE adoption. We have helped over 6000+ customers across various industries and geographies to transform to a cloud operating model through RISE with SAP among which 3000+ customers went live.

During this journey of cloud transformation, we have observed that it is essential for every enterprise to evolve with the latest technologies in order to adopt them and remain competitive in the market. To maintain the pace with the need for agility and innovation, while moving towards fast-changing technologies, customers need to adapt to changes that are evolving in AI technologies and innovations. As AI and Generative AI have become the top priorities for every enterprise striving to stay competitive and maintain its market position, there are a few challenges that all enterprises must acknowledge and overcome.

Below are the transformational drivers that are impacting customers to be prepared for the future.

  • Rising Customer Expectations: In the digital age, customers are more empowered than ever, driving the demand for exceptional experiences, personalized services, and seamless interactions across various channels.
  • Technological Advancements: The rapid emergence of technologies like generative AI presents both a challenge and an opportunity for businesses to stay ahead by leveraging innovation for growth.
  • Operational Efficiency: Enhancing operational efficiency is crucial for enterprises aiming to streamline processes, reduce costs, and boost productivity.
  • Competitive Pressure: To maintain a competitive edge and secure market position, companies must continuously evolve and adapt to the ever-changing landscape.

To address these challenges, SAP Cloud ERP is infused with AI capabilities to accelerate business ability and meet business goals.

McKinsey reports that Generative AI could generate over $2.6 to $4.4 trillion incremental value annually to the global market. As AI adoption accelerates, particularly in the area of Generative AI, there is an extreme need to capitalize on this technology. Leaders across industries are prioritizing investments and closely tracking the potential impact of Generative AI, recognizing that failure to stay competitive could pose significant risks. CEOs are making these strategic decisions to avoid losing market share and maintaining their competitive edge.

 

Why only cloud? And limitations of On-Premise:

The latest technological advancements like Generative AI need high computation power for efficient and accelerated performance. It requires a level of infrastructure that can handle the speed at which the innovations are changing and, run, activate, and deploy the AI use cases effectively. Data plays a critical role in implementing AI use cases, with the volume of data growing rapidly each day. To efficiently process and fine-tune this data for optimal and accelerated performance, the latest and most powerful infrastructure that can scale according to evolving requirements is essential.

The on-premise environments become challenging when scaling the systems to meet increased AI demand. The computation power for on-premise systems becomes costly and time-consuming. Significant resources and infrastructure are required to grow and maintain hardware and software systems. The on-premise environment presents significant challenges, making a cloud a more effective option considering scalability, speed, and cost-effectiveness.

Benefits to Customers 

By adopting AI, customers can gain benefits that can impact both their Business and People. In the field of business, AI enhances decision-making faster, with data-driven insights and recommendations, ensuring safer and more informed choices. For individuals, AI transforms work through automation and streamlines user experiences, ultimately driving better business outcomes

Benefits of AI for Business:

Reinvent Customer Service with personalized experiences that address customer needs quickly.

Boost Finance Excellence with AI that helps optimize working capital and closing functions.

Drive Operational Excellence by optimizing manufacturing and asset performance and elevating product quality.

Advance Intelligent Order Fulfillment by streamlining and automating the order fulfillment process.

Benefits of AI for People:

Empower End Users to increase efficiency and eliminate redundant tasks ​

Interact with Business using natural language for informational, navigational, and transactional assistance.​

Uncover Insights that would otherwise be challenging or time-consuming to discover 

 

SAP Business AI in RISE with SAP

Aligning with SAP’s Business AI strategy, SAP launched the integration of Joule starting with Success Factors in Dec 2023. Now, Joule is available for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud for RISE with SAP customers. Joule offers a navigational pattern helping in navigating to relevant SAP Fiori apps and letting the user work directly in the app’s web UI for new and occasional users without prior knowledge of individual SAP Fiori apps.

RISE customers will have the advantage of experiencing generative AI capabilities across SAP cloud ERP. All existing and new RISE customers will have access to Joule to quickly access apps, effectively gain insights on business objects, and speedily resolve user issues. There are a few prerequisites to access Joule on SAP S/4HANA quoted below:

Prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP S/4HANA (requires administrator role):

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01 or later.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform, with the following entitlements
    • Joule Entitlements - Joule(das-application), please refer to license topic
    • SAP Workzone, standard edition
    • SAP Cloud Connectivity
    • Cloud Foundry
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • SAP Cloud Connector with Principal Propagation
    • System Mapping to expose the required HTTP protocol

To know more about the capabilities of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition and complete the configuration guide follow this link.

Commercial Packages

As part of RISE with SAP offerings, entitlements are provided with different customer profile packages like Base Edition, Premium Edition, and Premium Plus Edition. Joule is accessible in all available packages Base, Premium, and Premium Plus for RISE customers. Joule will be accessible by all existing and new RISE customers fulfilling the prerequisites mentioned above to use capabilities. Joule and Generative AI will be part of the new SKU as entitlements under the Artificial Intelligence domain.

RISE Base Edition: Provides small organizations with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition functionalities with a lean setup

RISE Premium Edition: Enables organization’s business transformation supported by built-in AI capabilities into SAP applications, process automation, low-code business application development, and access to supplier networks.

RISE Premium Plus Edition: Extends premium by unlocking more AI scenarios, including sustainability solutions, and advanced finance capabilities.

AI has significant potential to accelerate business processes and optimize decision-making. Since AI is at the top of every enterprise’s priority list, we are constantly innovating to deliver more AI-powered business processes and bringing the capabilities of Business AI as an entitlement as part of the SKU for RISE with SAP. Generative AI capabilities are available for RISE Premium and RISE Premium Plus packages where respective AI units are available based on the package. RISE with SAP Premium customers are provided with AI units to pilot AI-driven innovations like SAP Cash Application, SAP Business Integrity Screening, and SAP Service Management.

SAP AI Unit is a single SKU. SAP’s AI use cases are commercialized as capabilities within a single SKU regardless of LoBs. All customers can license SAP AI Unit to purchase AI units but to be able to use specific AI capabilities, you need to fulfill the licensing pre-requisites.

AI units are like a virtual currency that allows you to consume a wide set of AI use cases across different LoB solutions within SAP which included Joule overages. AI units are based on annual subscriptions and are sold in a block of 100 AI units. The minimum block to purchase is 1 per year and must be used by the end of the year as all unused AI units will be lost if not consumed by the end of the year.

Commercials & Estimator

RISE customers will have entitlements of 2500 Joule messages per FUE per contract year free of charge to interact with SAP A/4HANA Cloud, private edition. Once the entitled number of messages is consumed and you are willing to continue accessing Joule, you can purchase additional messages with AI units SKU. A message is an electronic communication exchanged via the capability of cloud service. For cloud services like Joule, a user request, together with the corresponding response from Joule is considered as 1 message.

Customers can use the AI Unit Estimator to estimate x number of AI units required to run the selected AI capabilities and can request a quote from the estimator directly. After selecting the AI feature, the customer can add the number of transactions (in a block of 10,000) you would need for the selected AI feature. Cost estimation is automatically shown based on the selection and a quote can be created from the same page for the selected AI feature and the transactions based on the requirement.

Another way where customers can access the AI services is from the SAP for Me dashboard under the Portfolio & Products tab by selecting Business AI and activating the AI feature. The features are listed based on the SAP products they belong to, where customers can select whether to activate the feature or not. The customer has the option to view the consumption for the respective feature or calculate cost estimation for the feature by selecting an estimator for the respective feature.

Early Adopter Care (EAC) Program: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

SAP offers innovation adopter services to help you jump-start your projects with SAP’s latest products and solutions. SAP Early Adopter Care connects customers with products to help them simplify, innovate, and digitize fast. Through the support of the Early Adopter Care project coach, we enable two-way communication, and transparency, and allow stakeholders to minimize project risk.

Benefits of the EAC program:

Support with early implementation of SAP’s latest innovations.

Direct channel to development for product and solution insights.

To join the Early adopter care program for Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition, check out this link. Registration for the EAC program for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition started on 15th July and is open till 31st Dec 2024.

Conditions for participation:

Participation in the early adopter care program is voluntary, at the sole discretion of SAP, and dependent on the following requisites.

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01
  • Plans to use Joule in production
  • Willingness to become a reference
  • The full set of restrictions can be found here General Constraints for Joule & Constraints for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Customer Engagement Initiative: AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you, as an SAP customer or partner, to get insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

What is in for you?

  • Opportunity to define and shape the product's future.
  • Gain early insights and actively engage to learn and shape the new innovations that matter.
  • Access and engage directly with the product engineering teams.

How to get engaged with us?

Visit this link and register your company for this opportunity.

 

Joule Integration – Step-by-step guide

To configure Joule and integrate with SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, check the below blogs for a step-by-step guide.

Joule – Getting Started with Joule and SAP SuccessFactors

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

entry.title: Generative AI with SAP – Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Generative AI with SAP, which is focused on understanding how SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across its portfolio.

In this series we are learning:

  • How SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across it’s portfolio.
  • What SAP Business AI is and how SAP partners and customers can use it?
  • What is SAP AI Core? What is SAP AI launchpad? What is Joule? etc.
  • What role SAP BTP plays in SAP's AI strategy?
  • Hands-on, Real-life examples and many more.

To make it easy to understand, the blog series is divided in small parts. Each blog requires maximum 10–15 minutes to learn.

  • Part 1 – Introduction to SAP Business AI 
  • Part 2 – Components of SAP Business AI 
  • Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot 
  • Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP [Current blog]
  • Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core
  • Part 6 – Introduction to SAP AI launchpad
  • Part 7 – Get Started with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad
  • part 8 - Hello World using SAP AI Core
  • Part 9 – Overview of Generative AI Hub
  • Part 10 – SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
  • Part 11 - RAG, a real-life use case using SAP AI Core, Generative AI Hub and HANA Vector Engine

 

This is the 4th blog in the series, where will learn about AI Foundation.

Note: I will publish the subsequent  blogs soon.

 

Let’s first quickly revise what SAP BTP is

In January 2021, SAP introduced a unique and powerful solution offering called SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). SAP BTP is the technical foundation of entire SAP ecosystem and plays a crucial role for all SAP customers and partners.

In other word, SAP BTP is a portfolio of SAP solutions and services that are brought under one umbrella. These services, and solutions helps organizations build a new cloud solution or extend SAP systems.

For more information on SAP BTP, see Explaining SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to a Beginner

 

AI Foundation

Imagine you are developers/architect working for an SAP customer or partner.

  • You have been assigned to build a new application on SAP BTP or extend an existing SAP solution.
  • You need to incorporate AI capabilities into your application/extension.
  • To do so, you are looking for ready-to-use AI services and tools.
  • You want to make it simple and avoid individual contracts with 3rd party AI vendors and LLM providers.

AI foundation is an offering from SAP, which fulfil all these requirements.

 

So, what exactly is AI Foundation?

AI foundation on SAP BTP is a set of ready-to-use AI services and tools, which:

  • enable customers and partners (and SAP’s own development teams as well)
  • to create AI-powered extensions and applications
  • in a reliable, secure, and scalable way

 

In other words, AI Foundation is the set of AI services and tools on SAP BTP for developers to incorporate AI capabilities into applications/extensions.

Below image gives a high-level picture of AI foundation.

 

AI Foundation on SAP BTP provides an abstraction for SAP Customers and Partners

SAP has partnered with leading general-purpose AI vendors and large language model (LLM) providers to ensure that SAP customers are able to reap the benefit of AI innovations without going into individual contract with 3rd party vendors.

For example, if you need to use GPT LLM, you do not need to buy any separate license, you can access it via Generative AI Hub on SAP BTP. From Generative AI Hub, you can access these large language models from multiple vendors easily.

 

Components of AI Foundation

Below image, shows the major components of AI Foundation.

 

 

Let’s look into each of these components in detail.

 

1. AI services

AI services (aka AI Business Services) are ready-to-use, reusable AI services on SAP BTP.

  • Developers can use these AI services add AI capabilities into their application
  • These services include AI models pre-trained on business-relevant data
  • It covers wide range of business use cases.

 

These AI services are broadly categorized in 3 buckets as shown in below image.

 

Document Processing

AI foundation provides a ready-to-use service Document Information Extraction for intelligent document processing.

The Document Information Extraction service:

  • uses machine learning to automate information extraction processes from a document
  • helps to process large amounts of business documents that have content in headers and tables.

With Document Information Extraction service, we can:

  • Process more documents efficiently with fewer errors and difficulties
  • Increase quality and compliance mechanisms

We can use the extracted information to automate business processes. For example, automatically process payables, invoices, or payment notes while making sure that invoices and payables match.

 

Recommendation

There are 3 services under this bucket to enhance human-decision making:

  • Personalized Recommendation
  • Data Attribute Recommendation
  • Business Entity Recognition

 

Personalized Recommendation is a generic reusable service, which:

  • utilizes machine learning to provide customers personalized recommendations based on their browsing history and/or item description.
  • We can train and use machine learning models to deliver these recommendations across a wide range of business scenarios.

For example, SAP is leveraging this service to process more than 4.2 million learning recommendations for SAP SuccessFactors customers each month.

 

Data Attribute Recommendation service can be used if there are missing values in the dataset. The service:

  • uses free text, numbers and categories as input
  • to classify entities such as products, stores and users into multiple classes
  • and to predict the value of missing numerical attributes in your data records.

With Data Attribute Recommendation we can:

  • Automate and speed up data management processes
  • Reduce errors and manual efforts in data maintenance
  • Increase data consistency and accuracy

 

Business Entity Recognition is a service which helps to detect and highlight any type of business entity in unstructured text. For example, this service can be used to automatically extract the context from incoming emails with invoice inquiries, automating recurring tasks associated with answering queries about the status and payment of invoices.

 

Machine Translation

SAP is being used all over the world and in almost all the languages and that brings an important aspect of Translation of Texts to all the required languages. To accelerate and automate the translation process, SAP offers SAP Translation Hub service under AI Foundation.

SAP Translation Hub is a reusable service to translate content into multiple languages. This service helps you speed up the translation of software texts (e.g. user interfaces) and related documents – with high quality and accuracy. You can use a repository of SAP-approved translations and terminology as well as machine translation, which has been trained with focus on SAP-specific content.

Note: You can find the list of all the AI services and their details in SAP Discovery Center or you can visit the official product documentation.

 

How can SAP Customers/Partners use AI Services?

Customers and partner can integrate the SAP AI Services into their own processes and applications by connecting and calling the provided API endpoints accordingly.

Let’s understand this with an example.

 

Use Personalized Recommendation service to give visitors to a website personalized recommendation

Let’s say we want to use Personalized Recommendation service to give visitors to your website highly personalized recommendations based on their browsing history and/or item description.

Below diagram shows the high-level architecture diagram for this use-case.

 

We can achieve this as a sequence of steps shown below.

Note: You can follow this tutorial to get step-by-step guide for this entire process.

 

Similar to the approach we took for Personalized Recommendation, we can use other AI services as shown in below diagram.

 

Few more tutorials on SAP AI services

Use Machine Learning to Extract Information from Business Documents and Enrich Data

Use Pre-Trained Machine Learning Models to Process Unstructured Text

Shape Machine Learning to Process Custom Business Documents

Use Machine Learning to Classify Data Records

Use Generative AI to Process Business Documents

 

2. Generative AI Management

Second major component of AI Foundation is Generative AI Management. Under this bucket, AI foundation provides an offering called Generative AI Hub – which is an BTP service to explore different LLM models.

Let’s have a close look into Generative AI Hub.

 

What is Generative AI Hub?

 

 

Generative AI Hub helps us to fast-track development of any generative AI apps on SAP BTP. It enables developers to instantly access broad range of large language models (LLMs) from different providers, such as GPT-4 by Azure OpenAI or Open-Source Falcon 40B.

Generative AI – an BTP service to explore different LLM models.

 

We can use Generative AI Hub to orchestrate multiple generative AI models:

  • Either programmatically via SAP AI Core
  • or via the playground within SAP AI Launchpad

 

The generative AI hub also provides tooling for prompt engineering, experimentation, and other capabilities to speed-up the development of generative AI applications on BTP, in a secure and trusted way.

 

Below image shows a quick glimpse of how we can submit a prompt to multiple LLMs and compare the generated outcomes to identify the best-suited model using Generative AI Hub.

Disclaimer: The above image is taken from this blog

Note: For more information on Generative AI Hub, refer to Part 9 – Overview of Generative AI Hub [to be published]

 

AI Workload Management

AI workload management are targeted for developers who wants to build a new AI model from scratch or run an AI workflow or execute an AI asset in a standardized, scalable, and hyperscaler-agnostic way. AI workload management offers all the necessary tools to create AI models, train them, evaluate their accuracy and publish them for inferencing.

Under AI Workload Management bucket, AI foundation provides two services:

  • SAP AI Core
  • SAP AI Launchpad.

 

What is SAP AI Core?

SAP AI Core is a service available on SAP BTP, which offers a powerful AI Runtime. We can use SAP AI Core to train and deploy our AI models cost-efficiently at scale while preserving privacy and compliance.

One of the main benefits of SAP AI Core is that we can choose underlying infrastructure (GPUs, CPU Cores, Memory) from a broad range of plans, as well as avail built-in autoscaling. You can have a quick look on the various resource plan available for SAP AI Core here.

Note: For more information on SAP AI Core, refer to Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core

 

What is SAP AI Launchpad?

SAP AI Launchpad is a service on SAP BTP, which can be used to manage and run AI workflows and AI models across multiple AI runtimes (including SAP AI Core).

SAP AI Launchpad provides generative AI capabilities via the Generative AI Hub.

SAP AI launchpad also provides centralize AI lifecycle management for our AI scenarios with a convenient user interface. We can monitor model performance statistics continuously and retrain as needed.

Note: For more information on SAP AI Launchpad, refer to Part 6 – Introduction to SAP AI Launchpad [to be published]

 

Few Important point regarding SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

  • SAP AI Core is natively integrated with SAP AI Launchpad.
  • The SAP AI Launchpad offers an easy-to-use interface to manage AI workflow administration, processes, and tasks.
  • SAP AI Core can also be accessed by other supported tools as well, such as Postman or programmatically using Python.
  • Technically, SAP AI Launchpad is a multitenant software as a service (SaaS) application on SAP BTP.
  • AI Launchpad can be used to manage multiple AI runtime (e.g., multiple AI Core instances).

 

Business Data and Context

The most important differentiator of SAP system from other AI providers is that – SAP systems contains huge business data of customers. These business data is the key to leverage AI models to solve real business problems.

With solutions like SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Datasphere, SAP helps providing business data and context to the AI models. SAP HANA Cloud vector engine has specifically been designed for generative AI use-case.

 

What is SAP HANA Cloud vector engine?

The vector engine is a new addition to SAP HANA Cloud, a database-as-a-service delivered on SAP BTP. Using SAP HANA Cloud vector engine, we can ground AI with unique business data.

The SAP HANA Cloud vector engine supports efficient storage and processing of vector data. This means we can execute joins, apply filters, and perform selects by combining vector data with various data types, including transactional, spatial, graph, and JSON data, all within the same SQL environment.

 

Here are some key benefits of the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine:

  • Simplify the data architecture, management, and security with the storage of vectors and other enterprise data within the same database.
  • Interact with all types of data (including vectors) using SQL.
  • Gain new insights into data by combining spatial, graph, JSON, and custom SQLScript with vector-based queries.
  • Incorporate vector use cases in solutions with the SAP HANA Cloud clients (including Python), the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA (hana-ml), and CAP.

Note: For more information on SAP HANA Cloud vector engine, refer to Part 10 – SAP HANA Cloud vector engine [to be published]

 

SAP HANA Cloud also provide AI function libraries - Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) and Automated Predictive Library (APL). These libraries allow us to implement classification, regression or time series forecasting scenarios applied directly to the business data.

SAP also provides a solution, SAP Datasphere for data management. SAP Datasphere includes AI in several ways, for example in data integration, data quality & cleansing (such as correcting missing values or duplicates), data governance, analytics (such as detecting trends, analyze historical data, and make predictions), and natural language processing.

 

Foundation Model

Under AI Foundation umbrella, SAP BTP provides a wide range of foundation models that can be used to implement various generative AI use-cases. To provide a seamless experience to SAP customers, SAP has partnered with leading general-purpose AI vendors and LLMs providers, such as Google, Meta, IBM, Nvidia etc.

These foundation models, help ensure that SAP customers keep up with the fast pace of innovation and have the flexibility of choosing right model for their scenario.

 

What exactly is a foundation model?

A foundation model is a deep learning model which is trained on huge amount of data, usually with unsupervised learning. The model can be adapted to perform a wide range of tasks such as text generation, image generation, video generation, sentiment analysis, information extract etc.

Foundation models can be considered as general-purpose technologies that can support a diverse range of use cases. Since these models are pre-trained on vast datasets using powerful hardware and techniques, they can be used to save time and computational costs compared to training new models for each specific application.

Below image summarizes important points about foundation model.

To know more about foundation model, you may refer to What is Foundation Model?

 

SAP Foundation Models - SAP's own foundation model to redefine enterprise AI

Let’s first understand why customers need a foundation model from SAP.

Why general-purpose foundation models may not be enough for SAP customers?

There are many general-purpose foundation models available which are pre-trained on huge data. Under AI Foundation, SAP provides easy access to many of these models such as GPT-4, Llama2, Falcon-40b, Claude2 and many more. While these models offer amazing opportunities, they also have limitations. For example, LLMs may rely on outdated training data and lack company-specific data and business process context.

As an example, imagine LLM as a colleague who is very smart and knowledgeable. This colleague can answer most of your queries. However, he may not know what has happened in the world last year and he also does not have any idea about your company’s internal process and policies.

For example, let’s say you ask a LLM - “What do you think about the offer from our most important supplier last week?”.

An LLM can only work with the initial training data and may nor provide accurate answer.

However, if you provide enough business data and context to the LLM, then you can get the answer you expect.

 

Supplementing this lack of information is where SAP Foundation Model comes into the picture. SAP is trying to adapt the LLMs with the SAP business data and context to create it’s own models which will be best suited for customers.

 

So, what exactly is SAP foundation model?

SAP is designing its own foundation models by combining general-purpose large language models with its deep understanding of business processes and business data. These models will help customers to get context-specific response.

The SAP foundation model will be very useful in all core business areas such as finance, sales, supply chain, etc. It will be able to address questions we face every day in business that large language models cannot answer properly, such as predicting invoice payment dates and supplier delivery quality or proposing efficiency improvements to a business process.

SAP foundation model will also allow for fine-tuning for different scenarios, will further unlock and strengthen SAP’s leading position in embedded AI. Customers will benefit from high-performance models trained on rich SAP data, achieving better predictive results.

 

Please note that customers don’t have to wait for these SAP foundation models to be ready before they can leverage context from business data. There is another approach called Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), which also can be used to provide business data context and give customers better results right away.

Note: For more information on RAG, refer to Part 11 - RAG, a real-life use case using SAP AI Core, Generative AI Hub and HANA Vector Engine [to be published]

 

What’s Next?

Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core

Disclaimer– All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

entry.title: Generative AI boosting Process Automation? Come see our the results at SAP TechEd!
entry.content:
Where do we come from in Process Automation?

In a blog post I published 9 months ago, I told you the story of the Process Automation solution at SAP, from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build Process Automation.

Recently, Thomas Volmering explained how we are taking a big step forward by bringing Generative AI capabilities to process automation. In fact, we've made great progress this year by infusing GenAI into the 3 pillars that make up our Enterprise Automation suite: SAP Signavio, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Integration Suite.

I'm excited to tell you that you'll see the results of our efforts in a month's time at SAP TechEd.

Just for fun, I asked NightCafe Studio to create "a photorealistic image showing a crowd of software developers from all over the world. They are at SAP TechEd conference, attending a presentation on generative AI in Enterprise Automation solutions".  ➡️

What do you think of this image?

Tell me in the comments.

 

 

Here we are: Generative AI across Process Automation!

I invite you to register for our online event, and to watch the Launch Keynote – “Where ideas get real”: you will see and amazing end-to-end demo based on a real-life use case… but I for sure won’t spoil it. 😉

After that, I highly recommend watching Thomas Volmering and Alexander Rother from SAP, with Björn Schulz, from  REWE digital in session AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions.

They will present you a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can improve your development processes and drive business growth.

In addition, do not miss to add these sessions to your agenda:

XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio

Presented by Stephan Schluchter and Robin Kiwitt, this session will let you discover how enterprise automation combines the power of SAP Build solutions and SAP Integration Suite running on SAP BTP with SAP Signavio solutions. Explore how you can act based on process insights and rapidly transition from process modeling and simulation to executable actions. Learn how to achieve process optimization by leveraging the capability of BPMN models imported into SAP Build, and act on top of the process mining and modeling in SAP Signavio.

AD109 | Experience the next level of Enterprise Automation with SAP 

In this session presented by Palak Garg and Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan you will explore the latest enhancements using SAP Build solutions, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio solutions together.

Our experts will show you how to drive your AI-powered business transformation by combining process analysis, process automation, and integration capabilities. They will show you how to extend your approval processes with API-based integrations, and how you can leverage event-driven architectures, turn process insights and modeling outcomes into actions, and orchestrate your processes at scale across heterogenous landscapes.

AD110 | Accelerate development with the latest AI capabilities in SAP Build 

In this session with Marc Huber and Rupam Bhattacharya you will discover the power of Joule in SAP Build solutions to accelerate your development process and the AI features in SAP Build that improve user productivity.

Dive into the latest AI features designed for developers, enabling you to extend SAP S/4 HANA. Experience first-hand Gen AI-powered fusion development in SAP Build. Learn how to productively automate processes and workflow with AI capabilities. Get an overview of what's new and the latest in AI capabilities in SAP Build.

IN105 | Unleash the power of AI-driven enterprise automation with SAP solutions

With Palak Garg and Evgeniy Gorbunov, learn how to extend and integrate your business processes by combining SAP Integration Suite with SAP Build and SAP Signavio solutions, supported by AI capabilities and prebuilt content.

Future-proof your journey to become an intelligent enterprise using automation-based integration. Achieve the next level of integration efficiency and process improvement and drive innovation by infusing AI capabilities at scale, all while supporting it with the rich set of prebuilt content.

 

Join us at SAP TechEd 2024!

So if you're a process automation enthusiast, you now have your agenda to get the most out of SAP TechEd 2024. See you next month! 👍🏻 

➡️ Register now for SAP TechEd 2024 ✅

➡️ Join our Enterprise Automation initiative ✅

entry.title: Large Action Models (LAM) powered Agents for Enterprise Process Automation
entry.content:
Action AgentWith the rise of AI and machine learning, one of the most exciting developments in this space is the emergence of Large Action Models (LAMs). Remember this video of Satya Nadela with Bloomberg where he compared iPhone’s vision by Steve Jobs with that of Rabbit’s R1 device which essentially utilizes LAM to do tasks for you e.g.; book a flight or narrate direction. Well, maybe Rabbit didn’t go big with R1 yet, but Action Models got a hype that it deserves.

Since then, there have been many movements around this space, recently Adept.ai - a San Francisco based startup that had received $350 Million funding to build “agents” which automate enterprise workflows; its executives were hired by Amazon along with tech licensing so that Amazon can boost up its AI portfolio.

We think, these models and agents together represent a significant leap forward in the way we approach process automation, particularly within the context of SAP and enterprise systems.

Now it’s time for Action!

What is a Large Action Model (LAM)?

A Large Action Model (LAM) is a sophisticated machine learning model designed to understand, predict, and automate complex tasks across various domains. Unlike traditional AI models that focus on specific tasks (e.g., natural language processing, image classification, sentiment analysis), LAMs are designed to predict sequences of (complex) actions that can be executed in a real-world system with minimal human intervention.

LAMs are trained on vast amounts of data, data in this case is not numbers, texts, but it encompasses diverse workflows and operational scenarios. This allows these models to generalize across different processes, making them highly adaptable to various enterprise applications.

What is an AI Agent?

AI Agents autonomously execute tasks by using tools and managing resources, adapting their actions based on the context. With built-in memory, they retain knowledge from past interactions to improve future performance and decision-making.

The Large Action Model provides a framework for an autonomous agent to select and execute actions based on its current state and the desired outcome. Thus, LAM functions like a brain for an agent in the planning phase.

 

The Role of LAMs and Agents in SAP

SAP systems are the backbone of many large enterprises, managing everything from supply chain operations to human resources and financials. However, as you know best, ERP systems often involve complex, multi-step processes that require manual intervention, even with existing automation tools.

This is where LAMs powered Agents come into play. By utilizing these agents into SAP environments and processes, we believe enterprises can achieve a higher level of process automation and gain efficiency like never before. Here’s how:

  1. End-to-End Process Automation: LAMs can automate entire business processes that span multiple SAP modules. For example, in a procure-to-pay process, a LAM can handle everything from purchase requisition creation to invoice processing, reducing the need for human oversight.

     

  2. Adaptive Workflows: Traditional automation tools require predefined rules and conditions (e.g., RPAs). LAMs, on the other hand, can learn and adapt to changing business environments. They can modify workflows on the fly, ensuring that SAP processes remain efficient even as business needs evolve.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: With the integration of LAMs, the user experience within SAP systems can be significantly improved. LAMs can provide real-time assistance to users, guiding them through complex processes, or even taking over routine tasks entirely, allowing employees to focus on higher-value activities.

And in future, when SAP data is exposed to LAMs; then agents can not only execute process steps but could also help with  Predictive Analytics and Decision-Making: Here LAMs can predict potential issues or forecast values within SAP workflows before their occurrence. For instance, by analysing historical data, a LAM can predict number of days the order will be delayed or forecasts the price in the order without user needing to fill in.

Some examples in SAP where LAM’s can bring high value

  1. Intelligent Financial Close:
  • Financial closing is a critical process for any enterprise, often involving multiple SAP modules and significant manual effort. LAMs can automate the entire closing process, from reconciling accounts to generating financial statements, ensuring accuracy and speed.
  1. Supply Chain Optimization:
  • In supply chain management, LAMs can be used to automate demand forecasting, inventory management, and supplier coordination.  
  1. Human Capital Management (HCM):
  • LAMs can transform how HR processes are managed within SAP systems. For example, they can automate employee onboarding, performance reviews, and even handle complex payroll calculations, ensuring compliance with local regulations.
  1. Customer Relationship Management (CRM):
  • In the CRM space, LAMs can automate customer interactions, from initial contact to post-sale support. They can analyze customer data to provide personalized recommendations, automate follow-ups, and even predict customer churn, allowing for proactive engagement.

Conclusion

Large Action Models powered Agents represent the next frontier in process automation for SAP and the broader enterprise world. By leveraging this, businesses can achieve unprecedented levels of efficiency, accuracy, and agility in their operations. As we move forward, we are looking for input and feedback so that we can shape this innovation in context of SAP data and processes.

Leave your comments to share your thoughts!

  • We need your input on use-cases, workflows and processes in SAP that you think will benefit the most!
  • For usability - Would you see automation of process steps happening in background or in front so that you can review it?
  • Some brainstorming on what marks the successful execution of process execution by AI?
  • What happens in case of exception and edge cases when some specific condition deviates the current run of execution from the typical flow of process steps which LAM is not aware of? Say for example a threshold limit triggers a custom-built popup on UI that the model has not seen before.
  • How much input do you think is sufficient to give upfront that LAM is able to fulfil E2E execution of process flow without being stuck and waiting for user input. For example, mandatory fields in the UI that can not be pre-determined via a logic but can only be provided by end user.

So, are you ready to explore how SAP’s Large Action Models can revolutionize your SAP processes?  

Take Action

entry.title: Generative AI with SAP – Part 5: Introduction to SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Generative AI with SAP, which is focused on understanding how SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across its portfolio.

In this series we are learning:

  • How SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across it’s portfolio.
  • What SAP Business AI is and how SAP partners and customers can use it?
  • What is SAP AI Core? What is SAP AI launchpad? What is Joule? etc.
  • What role SAP BTP plays in SAP's AI strategy?
  • Hands-on, Real-life examples and many more.

To make it easy to understand, the blog series is divided in small parts. Each blog requires maximum 10–15 minutes to learn.

  • Part 1 – Introduction to SAP Business AI 
  • Part 2 – Components of SAP Business AI 
  • Part 3 – Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot 
  • Part 4 – AI Foundation on SAP BTP 
  • Part 5 – Introduction to SAP AI Core [Current blog]
  • Part 6 – Introduction to SAP AI launchpad
  • Part 7 – Get Started with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad
  • part 8 - Hello World using SAP AI Core
  • Part 9 – Overview of Generative AI Hub
  • Part 10 – SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
  • Part 11 - RAG, a real-life use case using SAP AI Core, Generative AI Hub and HANA Vector Engine

 

This is the 5th blog in the series, where will learn about SAP AI Core.

Note: I will publish the subsequent  blogs soon.

 

Before understanding SAP AI Core, it might be a good idea to quickly break down few important terminologies to understand SAP AI Core better.

 

What is an AI Model?

An AI Model is a computer program that has been trained to perform specific tasks by learning from data. The AI models (programs) usually include complex mathematical and computational techniques to process vast amounts of data and extract meaningful insights.

Below image summarizes important points about AI Model.

 

AI models need huge datasets to get trained. The training data may include text, images, videos, numbers, or any other format of data. Some powerful AI models are trained on entire Internet data.

To know more about AI Models, you may refer to the blog What is AI Model?

 

What is AI Runtime?

AI Runtime refers to the environment or platform where AI models or algorithms are executed.

It includes the necessary software, tools, and resources needed to run AI models in production, such as hardware (CPUs, GPUs), software frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch), and supporting services (like inference servers).

AI Runtimes are used to deploy, manage, and run AI models efficiently. 

Examples of AI Runtime:

  • TensorFlow Serving
  • Amazon SageMaker
  • Google AI Platform
  • Azure Machine Learning
  • SAP AI Core

 

What do we mean by AI Workflows?

In general, Workflow is defined as a sequence of tasks that processes a set of data through a specific path from initiation to completion. Workflows are the paths that describe how something goes from being undone to done, or raw to processed.

AI workflows are the workflows which use AI to streamline and improve business processes that are often manual and repetitive.

 

What is Inference Server?

Large language models, once trained, need to be deployed so they can be used to make predictions or generate text in real-time. This is where inference servers come into play.

An inference server hosts the trained model and handles requests from users or applications to generate outputs based on new input data.  

In other words, we can say - An inference server is a specialized server designed to deploy and run machine learning models, particularly for making predictions or inferences in real-time. 

Example of Inference Server

NVIDIA Triton Inference Server is a scalable and flexible inference server for deploying AI models at scale. Triton Inference Server can host and serve models like GPT-2 and GPT-3.

 

Now, let’s come to the main topic – SAP AI Core!

 

What is SAP AI Core?

SAP AI Core is a service available on SAP BTP, which offers a powerful AI Runtime. We can use SAP AI Core to run AI workflows and AI models.

SAP AI Core is designed to handle the execution and operations of AI assets in a standardized, scalable, and hyperscaler-agnostic way.

 

Some Important point on SAP AI Core

  • SAP AI Core is SAP’s runtime for heavy-load AI. It allows us to train and deploy AI models cost-efficiently at scale.
  • SAP AI Core is natively integrated with SAP AI Launchpad.
  • The SAP AI Launchpad offers an easy-to-use interface to manage AI workflow administration, processes, and tasks.
  • SAP AI Core can also be accessed by other supported tools, such as Postman or programmatically using Python.
  • SAP AI Core supports full lifecycle management of AI scenarios.

 

Features and Capabilities of SAP AI Core

The main capabilities that SAP AI Core provides are the orchestration of AI workflows, such as model trainings and batch inference, as well as serving model inference, so that models can make predictions. Below diagram summarizes various features and functionalities of SAP AI Core.

 

 

Let’s demystify the above diagram!

 

Standardized AI Interface

The AI models running on SAP AI Cores can be easily integrated with any business applications with the help of a standardized AI interface. These standardized AI interface (called AI API), helps us integrate AI models as well as managed, monitored, and operated SAP AI Core with SAP AI Launchpad (or even Postman or Python).

 

What Exactly is AI API?

To connect to the business applications as well as to operate our AI scenarios in AI Launchpad, SAP AI Core provides the standardized AI interface, called AI API.

The AI API is not a product but a unified API definition, which provides a common framework for consuming and operating our AI scenarios. With this unified API definition, we benefit from unified consumption from business applications and unified operations and management in SAP AI Launchpad.

In other words, AI API provides an abstraction layer for managing AI asset, scenarios and workflows in SAP AI Core.  

For more information on AI API, refer this document or check AI API Reference.

 

Full Lifecycle Management

SAP AI Core provides full lifecycle management support such as content deployment using the GitOps principle. It is built around state-of-the-art open source solutions such as Argo Workflows and KFServing.

 

Choose your own infrastructure

One of the main benefits of SAP AI Core is that it allows us to choose from a broad range of storage, CPU and GPU service plans.

We can configure SAP AI Core to use different infrastructure resources for different tasks, based on demand. To achieve this, SAP AI Core provides several preconfigured infrastructure bundles called “resource plans” for this purpose. This document provides the list of existing resource plans.

 

Simplifies AI development using SAP managed deployments

With SAP AI Core, we benefit from SAP-managed model deployments, which hide the complexity of managing deployments. We can just expose an API endpoint that can be integrated and consumed from a business application. SAP AI Core exposes simple API endpoints to embed AI into your business applications.

 

Perfect balance between costs and performance

With SAP AI Core, on the one hand, we benefit from accelerated performance with GPU support to run most resource-hungry use cases at scale. On the other hand, we can run efficiently while keeping control of the costs by leveraging built-in autoscaling and scale-to-zero as well as by choosing from a broad range of storage, CPU, and GPU service plans.

Note: Scale-to-zero is a concept often used in cloud computing, particularly in the context of AI.
