It means that an application/service, or AI model is automatically turned off or scaled down to use zero computing resources when it is not in use to save cost. When a request comes in, the system automatically "scales up" by starting up again, processing the task, and then scaling back down to zero once the task is complete.

 

Easily embed AI capabilities in SAP applications and business processes

With SAP AI Core, we can take advantage of the standardized integration into SAP applications to quickly build and integrate AI use cases into business applications.

 

CI/CD and Multitenancy Support

To ease the shipment of new AI scenarios, SAP AI Core offers continuous delivery capabilities and ensures tenant isolation with multi-tenancy. To safeguard customer’s costs and to scale on demand, we only need to pay for the resources we use but can tap into scalability and increased performance powered by GPUs.

All of the functionality comes out of the box and is managed by SAP while retaining openness to any AI framework so that customers can ship their AI scenario easily.

 

Development Tools – Libraries and SDKs

SAP AI Core provides development tools, such as SAP AI Core SDK to simplify the development process.

The SAP AI Core SDK is a Python-based SDK that lets you access SAP AI Core using Python methods and data structures. It provides tools that help you to manage your scenarios and workflows in SAP AI Core.

You can check the SAP AI Core SDK project at https://pypi.org/project/ai-core-sdk/

 

I hope you have got a clear understanding of what SAP AI Core is and it’s features. For more information on SAP AI Core, you may refer to SAP AI Core.

If you have reached so far, don't forget to give a kudos 😉

 

What’s Next?

Part 6 – Introduction to SAP AI launchpad [To be published soon]

entry.title: SAP AI - Certifications, Free Courses, Sample Projects and Many More!
entry.content:
Some of the common queries I receive on LinkedIn are –

  • How to get certified in SAP Business AI?
  • Are there any FREE courses by SAP on these topics?
  • Is there any sample project provided by SAP on AI?
  • Are there any sample codes which I can reuse to build by AI applications?

In this blog, I have tried to compile all these resources for your quick reference.

 

Note

If you are looking for a simple, step-by-step guide to learn SAP Business AI, you may refer to Generative AI with SAP Blog Series. If you are new to generative AI, I will  recommend you to spend few minutes reading the blog series Generative AI for Beginners and 21 Generative AI Jargons Simplified.

 

SAP AI Certifications

Currently, there is one FREE course by SAP, which provides you a Digital Badge as a Record of Achievement.

Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

In this course, you will learn SAP's Business AI strategy, its principles, ethics, privacy standards, and the opportunities it presents for your business.

 

FREE Courses by SAP on AI and SAP Business AI

Learning how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP Business Technology Platform

In this course, you will learn how to use SAP AI Core to handle the execution and operation of your artificial intelligence assets.

Generative AI at SAP

This course will help you understand the fundamental uses and benefits of AI in a business context. You will be able to understand the types and value of AI and engage more successfully with AI capabilities in SAP products and solutions.

Navigating Large Language Models fundamentals and techniques for your use case

This course will provide you a description of Large Language Models (LLMs) and SAP's Business AI strategy towards LLMs along with techniques and methods for optimal LLM performance for your use case.

 

SAP Discovery Mission – Guided Projects with Step-by-step Instructions and Best Practices

SAP provides these SAP Discovery Mission which includes not just guided projects, but also the reference architecture, step-by-step instructions, resources, best practices, support details and many more.

Here is the list of AI related missions.

Get Started with SAP Business AI

This mission guides you on how to onboard yourself on SAP Business AI.

Get Started with SAP Build Code and Joule using Generative AI (Trial and Enterprise Account)

This mission guides you through the setup of SAP Build Code and shows how to create your first full-stack app in SAP Build Code using Joule and Generative Artificial Intelligence.

Set Up SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub

This mission guides you in setting up SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub.

Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors

This mission guides you to setup SAP Joule with your SAP SuccessFactors instance.

Harnessing Generative AI Capabilities with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine

This mission guides you to leverage the capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Generative AI Hub, Azure OpenAI and the Langchain Framework.

Get Started with Document Information Extraction and Generative AI

In this mission, you will learn how generative AI can help you automate use cases for business document processing with LLMs (Large Language Models)

Digitizing Criticality Assessments using SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

This mission is based on a real customer use case about criticality assessments which is currently in productization.

Develop a CAP-based (multitenant) application using GenAI and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)

This mission will guide you on how to build a multitenant application using CAP, leveraging Generative AI Hub and HANA vector engine to implement RAG.

 

Reference Architectures

Retrieval Augmented Generation and Generative AI on SAP BTP

In this reference architecture pattern (multitenant, single tenant, how to seamlessly combine various Large Language Models (LLMs) using the Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core.

Federated Machine Learning

This reference architecture relates to the use case pattern "Federated Machine Learning" of the use case category "Predictive Analytics" as defined by the SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology.

 

Sample Codes on SAP Business AI

Social Media Citizen Reporting using Generative AI Hub

Embedding business context with the SAP HANA Cloud, Vector Engine

Bringing Open-sourced LLMs into SAP AI Core

Generative AI-based code development with Joule in SAP Build Code

 

Additional resources

SAP AI Community

SAP TechEd Group

SAP Learning Journeys

Liked it? Don't forget to give a kudos!

Hope this blog will be helpful for you. If I have missed anything important, please let me know in comment 😊

entry.title: SAP Build Code Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024
entry.content:
Hello Everyone !

That time of year is here again, SAP TechEd 2024 is just around the corner! 

 

SAP TechEd is the leading tech conference for all developers, IT professionals, business experts, tech visionaries, innovators, and leaders eager to gain in-depth knowledge of SAP’s solutions, platforms, and technologies, along with insights into their future direction. It also offer an opportunity to network and connect with peers and SAP experts. 

SAP TechEd in 2024 will kick off with a free, engaging virtual event accessible to everyone, followed by SAP TechEd on Tour stops all around the world for a unique opportunity for more in-depth, in-person learning experiences. The series of local on-site events will be organized in partnership with and hosted by SAP User Groups and SAPinsider. Learn more about the virtual experience here.

The choice is yours: Join us virtually for the first event in the series, SAP TechEd Virtual 2024! 

📌Save the date and register now!
📅 October 8 - 9, 2024

At this year's SAP TechEd, you will have the opportunity to attend a variety of sessions and formats - such as lectures, jumpstarts, and roadmaps - organized into six (6) content tracks that are aligned with your business needs, covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General (XP)
  • Application Development and Automation (AD)
  • Data and Analytics (DA)
  • Integration (IN)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)

 

📚Access the full session catalog of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024

 

SAP Build Code - Sessions

 

📅Tuesday, Oct 8

 

1. AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions

[Track Overview, 45 min] Discover a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can enhance your development processes and drive business growth.
Speakers: Dr. Alexander Rother (SAP) , Bjorn Schulz (SAP) , Andre Borchert (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 6:00 PM – 6:45 PM CEST​

 

2. AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.
Speakers: Tim Back (SAP) , Anne Keller (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 7:00 PM – 7:25 PM CEST​

 

📅Wednesday, Oct 9

 

3. AD104 | SAP Build Code: AI development and new service integrations with CAP

[Lecture, 25 min] Examine new capabilities of SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) in SAP Build Code, and experience how the Joule copilot can speed up your extension development. In addition, find out how service integrations with CAP can reduce development effort, help you avoid pitfalls, and allow for evolution without disruption following the clean core strategy.
Speakers: David Kunz (SAP) , Daniel Schlachter (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 11:30 AM – 11:55 AM CEST​

 

4. AD101 | Tailor your UI with SAPUI5 in SAP Build Code

[Lecture, 25 min] Discover the latest on SAPUI5 development in SAP Build Code, providing a modern user experience across ABAP and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Learn how to realize clean core extensibility with new capabilities and how you can future-proof your app with new tools and best practices. Understand why SAP Web Components are strategic and how you can benefit from using them.
Speakers: Oliver Graeff (SAP) , Peter Muessig (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 12:00 PM – 12:25 PM CEST​

 

5. AD103 | Extend your SAP solutions with custom mobile apps using SAP Build Code

[Lecture, 25 min] Leverage our mobile development kit, SAP BTP SDK for iOS, and SAP BTP SDK for Android to create extensible enterprise-grade mobile apps. Learn how you can transform your user experience with SAP Mobile Services and AI capabilities in SAP Build Code.

Speakers: Sami Lechner (SAP) , Sandeep Tetali (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 12:30 PM – 12:55 PM CEST​

 

📅Thursday, Oct 10

 

6. AD181 | Develop applications and processes with SAP Build and Joule

[Jump-Start, 25 min] Immerse yourself in creating comprehensive intelligent applications using SAP Build solutions in this hands-on session. Gain comprehensive knowledge of full-stack development, process automation, and business site creation with SAP Build. Learn how SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is leveraged in a highly productive development environment with Joule technology.

Speakers: Eric Solberg (SAP) , Patrick Schad (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Thursday, Oct 10 | 🕛 9:00 AM – 9:25 AM CEST​

 

 

I hope this overview gives you a few more reasons to join us at SAP TechEd 2024.

We look forward to meeting and interacting with you virtually at SAP TechEd Virtual and in person at the local events in West Palm Beach (US), Melbourne (AUS), Copenhagen (DK), Birmingham (UK), and Wiesbaden (DE).

In the meantime, you can check out this overview of the SAP Build Code Session at Devtoberfest which will take place virtually from Sept 23 to Oct 25, 2024.

Stay tuned - and see you there!

entry.title: Embedding Generative AI into SAP S/4HANA: A Holistic Approach to Intelligent Business Operations
entry.content:
In the fast-paced world of enterprise technology, Generative AI is making waves by offering capabilities far beyond traditional AI. In SAP S/4HANA, this evolution is driving a new era of transactional intelligence where AI not only generates text or automates workflows but actively engages with real-time data and enterprise processes to enhance business operations.

In this post, we’ll explore how Generative AI is embedded into SAP S/4HANA, transforming how businesses interact with their core systems, from automating content creation to making informed transactional decisions. This is based on the latest advancements highlighted by SAP’s own Generative AI solution concepts and expanding use cases​.

Generative AI: Moving from Content to Transactions

Generative AI traditionally started as a tool for text generation, handling tasks like content creation, summarization, or code generation. However, in the context of SAP, Generative AI’s role has expanded dramatically.

We now see GenAI being applied to a wider range of processes:

  1. Text -> Code -> Workflow -> Transactional Data: Generative AI in SAP now supports generating not just textual outputs but handling workflows and interacting with transactional data. Whether it's automating invoice generation, managing inventory, or creating product orders, GenAI can handle the entire flow autonomously, offering solutions to business challenges as they arise​.
  2. Prompting Large Language Models (LLMs) for Enhanced Business Interaction:
    • LLM Retrieval via Fine-Tuning and RAG: Through Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), businesses can now send SAP data into LLMs via HANA vector databases to receive precise responses based on real-time transactional information. Imagine asking the system, "Can this sales order be fulfilled by the end of the month?" and getting a direct, data-backed response​.
    • Document Processing through OCR and LLMs: GenAI can take unstructured data, such as scanned PDFs, and transform it into structured formats like JSON, automating document management processes like invoice extraction or contract analysis​.

These advancements enable non-technical users to interact with SAP’s intelligent core using natural language prompts, making complex business operations more intuitive and efficient.

Expanding LLM Capabilities for SAP S/4HANA

One of the most exciting advancements is the ability to extend LLMs to act as intelligent agents within the SAP environment. LLMs are no longer passive tools but can now plan, execute, and optimize business processes autonomously.

Key innovations include:

  1. LLM-Driven SAP API Integration: Generative AI is integrated with SAP APIs, allowing LLMs to autonomously retrieve API data, plan business actions (such as initiating a production order), and even execute these actions without human intervention​.
    • Example: The LLM accesses the SAP API_CORE, retrieves available APIs, and autonomously configures business processes such as setting up an order, managing inventory, or scheduling deliveries. This level of intelligent automation reduces manual intervention and accelerates business decisions.
  2. Four-Tier LLM Structure for Enhanced Enterprise Automation:
    • General-purpose LLMs: Handle generic language and data tasks.
    • Domain-specific LLMs: Fine-tuned with SAP data, these models can understand complex enterprise data and processes.
    • Cross-task LLMs: Perform more specialized functions like job posting creation, purchase order generation, and workflow automation.
    • Task-specific LLMs: Fully customized for specific business tasks, such as generating API calls for unique operational needs​(Embedding Generative AI…).

This hierarchical model structure offers both broad and specialized AI capabilities, empowering businesses to customize their AI solutions for optimal performance. A detailed blog is available on LAM (Large Action Model) powered agents for enterprise process automation to execute a bunch of tasks.

Building SAP’s Own Generative AI Foundation Models

SAP is moving toward building its own Generative AI foundation models, specifically designed to handle the unique challenges of enterprise environments. These models are not just about handling text or data—they integrate business process context, industry-specific knowledge, and transactional intelligence.

SAP’s own foundation models are trained on structured data, such as financial transactions, sales orders, and manufacturing records, as well as tabular and graph-based data. This integration allows businesses to query real-time transactional data for more accurate, predictive decision-making.

For example:

  • Predictive Transactional Intelligence: Imagine asking the system, “Will this product shipment arrive on time based on current production schedules?” SAP’s GenAI model, trained on past transactional data and current operational insights, will deliver a reliable prediction​.

SAP’s approach focuses on integrating these foundation models with business process metadata, creating a knowledge graph that continuously improves AI accuracy and relevance in real-time business contexts.

Solution Architecture: Embedding Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA

SAP’s Generative AI solution architecture is built on a scalable and adaptable framework. The architecture leverages SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to provide advanced capabilities in prompt engineering, embedding lookup, and model fine-tuning, all of which are key to customizing the AI to the needs of each enterprise.

Key components of the architecture include:

  1. Joule Integration: Joule is a natural language interface that helps users retrieve information or execute tasks through Conversational AI. The assistant interacts with the Generative AI models to provide real-time business insights​. A detailed blog will be available soon on Joule implementation.
  2. Advanced Prompting: For applications requiring complex prompt engineering, SAP’s BTP provides capabilities such as embedding domain-specific knowledge or leveraging vector search engines to improve the relevance of AI outputs. This ensures that the prompts provided to the LLMs are fine-tuned to yield the most accurate responses​.

Model Retraining for Custom Business Needs: SAP’s architecture supports model retraining using specific enterprise data. This allows businesses to continuously improve their AI models to match evolving operational requirements​.

Conclusion: Generative AI and the Future of SAP S/4HANA

The integration of Generative AI into SAP S/4HANA marks a turning point in the evolution of enterprise AI. With SAP building its own foundation models, capable of handling not just content but transactional and predictive business intelligence, the potential for businesses to automate, optimize, and innovate has never been greater.

From intelligent document processing to real-time decision-making and workflow automation, Generative AI is not only enhancing how enterprises operate but is paving the way for the future of the intelligent enterprise.

If you haven’t explored the potential of embedding Generative AI into your SAP environment, now is the time. Whether you're looking to automate processes, improve decision-making, or simply enhance user experiences, SAP S/4HANA is ready to help you unlock the full potential of this transformative technology.

entry.title: AD104 - Next steps
entry.content:
In our Teched session AD104 you have learned about how to build CAP applications using SAP Build Code. And how to enrich this application with prebuilt functionality and service integrations by utilizing CAP Plugins.

After entering into the development environment from the SAP Build Code Lobby, we used generative AI by providing it with a textual description of our use case. Joule AI used this input and generated very useful artefacts for us:

  • A complete CDS data model
  • A CDS service definition
  • Fiori Elements UI annotations
  • Sample data

If you think of a typical 3 tier architecture everything is covered. From the CDS data model the CAP framework automatically creates the database statements covering the database layer. The CAP application itself is the server and with our CDS service definition we have defined the behavior of the server and the endpoints it provides (by default via Odata v4). And the UI annotations provide the information to render a Fiori Elements User Interface that matches our model.

So one simple command created a complete full stack application, that we can start up an try out.

And with the sample data (which even fits the business context!) we can try out the application with meaningful test data.

This is a great starting point - and we can start to enhance the application now with additional functionalities and our business logic.

One common requirement across applications is uploading attachments. On our incidents sample app, it would be very helpful to upload supporting documents. Previously, that would have been a challenge connected with a ton of custom code, maintenance and so on. Maybe a thousand lines of code to access low level APIs of an attachment service, where you need to take care of everything. Plus the additional logic - what is the maximum attachment size? How to malware scan it? How to implement the UI to upload and download attachments.

Image instead a single dependency to a reusable package and two lines of code - that includes all of the above already.

That's the power of CAP Plugins and the story behind "Calesi" - CAP level service interfaces.

___

If you want to try out this functionality, the Joule functionalities are now available in SAP Build Code on a trial account.

The instructions for this can be found here:

https://developers.sap.com/mission.sap-build-code-test-drive.html

___

For a list of officially supported CAP plugins, check out the corresponding page in our documentation:

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/plugins/

For node.js there are two github organizations where all the plugins can be found:

cap-js : https://github.com/search?q=topic%3Aplugin+org%3Acap-js&type=Repositories

cap-js-community: https://github.com/cap-js-community

For a Devtoberfest session, where you can follow along hands on and learn how to add additional plugins and a custom handler to a Joule generated app, see https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4o2Swj89zxU 

___

Any more questions, feedback and so on - feel free to leave a comment!

Thank you for reading 😃

entry.title: Real Talk with Katya and Jerome | Artificial Intelligence Track Leads
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

We’re Katya Reznik and Jerome Simonetti, and we’re excited to introduce ourselves and give you a glimpse of what to expect from the AI track sessions at this year’s TechEd. 

Katya: Originally from Russia, I’ve lived in Israel for the past 9 years. I love wandering in the desert, snowboarding, diving in cenotes and seas, and exploring nature. I travel a lot, learn languages, make pottery, enjoy the rain, love all dogs and people with a sense of humor. I’m part of the Business AI unit and often talk about technologies and our future:) 

Jerome: I’m Product Expert at SAP Business AI Product Management team. I like to go on multi-day hikes with my wife. My vacation is a subtle balance between visiting cities and museums and going hiking. In terms of art, I'm quite eclectic. I love music (jazz, lyrical, opera) and I regularly go to concerts. I love painting, especially the Italian grandmasters of the 15th and 16th century.  I also like comics. I am married. I have 3 children who are adults. 2 of them have children! 

With our AI track you will learn how innovations like Joule, our generative AI copilot, and Large Language Models (LLMs) are transforming key areas such as finance, HR, supply chain, and IT. Discover real-world success stories, expert insights, and actionable ways to enhance decision-making and agility in your organization. 

We look forward to sharing insights and innovations in AI with you at TechEd! 

What inspired you to specialize in this track? 

The transformative potential of AI and how it can revolutionize the way businesses operate is truly fascinating. It’s an exciting opportunity to dive deep into topics of generative AI, machine learning, knowledge graphs and much more. Specializing in this track allows us to explore/witness real-world success stories and expert insights. 

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

This track perfectly captures the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit by offering both hands-on experience and real-world applications. The two jump-start sessions bring AI from theory to practical implementation: Set up Joule to answer your own policy documents and Build your own generative AI scenario for your use case. Additionally, our sessions with customers like Sappi and the City of Hamburg and a partner MSG showcase how AI is already driving innovation and delivering results in real business environments. 

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar): 

  • AI100 - Artificial intelligence that creates real-world results 
  • AI105 - Examining how to onboard and extend Joule 
  • AI106 - Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and generative AI hub 
  • AI104 - Revolutionize ABAP development with Joule, our generative AI copilot 
  • AI108 - How AI agents can support your business in the future 
  • AI102 - Enhance business predictions with SAP Foundation Model and knowledge graphs 
  • AI101 - Enable more secure and responsible generative AI 

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024? 

  • Curiosity & Questions: Most importantly, come with an open mind and questions. TechEd is about learning, so actively participate in sessions, ask the experts, and explore new ideas. 
  • Time Management: With so many sessions, it's easy to get overwhelmed. Build your agenda and don’t forget to schedule breaks to keep yourself fresh and engaged throughout the day. 
  • Comfortable Workspace ️: Since TechEd is virtual, set up a comfortable and quiet workspace that helps you focus. A good chair and reliable internet are important to stay connected throughout the sessions. 

We can’t wait to have you join us at TechEd Virtual! Together, we’ll explore how AI can transform business operations and create real impact. Register now, and let’s shape the future of AI together! We’re excited to engage with you, answer your questions, and learn from each other along the way. See you there!

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

As we wrap up another exciting month, we wanted to take a moment to share some of the latest updates with you. With September coming to a close, we are getting ready for two major events in the SAP Community - Devtoberfest and TechEd. For more information, be sure to read the rest of this release information.

 

Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic

You can use our generative AI assistant Joule in SAP Build Code to help you to write code quickly and easily by providing natural language prompts. As you know, we are constantly listening to your feedback to make our tools even better. One request we got was to enable AI support for external service scenarios. We are excited to tell you that it is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.

You start by creating your full-stack application, importing an external SAP S/4HANA Cloud service, and defining the data model and services. (With Joule, you can easily create your data model and services.)

Data Model and Service creation with the help of Joule

With our new feature, it is now possible to use Joule to enhance the data model you created before: You can add associations between the external entity and local entities, and you can expose the external entity to a local service via a projection.

Using Joule to add an association between the entity Books and the external entity A_BusinessPartner

We've also added support for custom logic generation to read the associated external SAP S/4HANA Cloud entity and to manipulate the exposed external entity.

Using Joule to add a custom logic reading data from the external entity A_BusinessPartner

 

SQL Notebooks for SAP HANA

SAP Build Code offers notebook functionality, similar to the well-known Jupyter notebooks. Since Q3 2024 the SAP HANA tools in SAP Build Code offer a notebook experience for executing SQL statements on an SAP HANA Cloud database.

To get started, create an SAP HANA dev space. Inside the dev space, create a file with the .notebook extension and open it. The file will be opened with the default notebook editor. Next, create a code block. The default code block type should be “SAP HANA Connection Management”. This code block type allows opening, closing, and selecting a database connection. Typing ‘#’ opens the value help with the available commands.

Choose the “List database connections” Command

Choose the “List database connections” command and execute the code block. A list of the available database connections from the SAP HANA Database Explorer view will be shown. You can choose a database connection to use for the notebook by clicking on its name in the list. The selected database will be highlighted with a different background color.

Select Database Connection

Now you can create another code block for executing SQL statements. Be sure to change the code block type to “SAP HANA SQL” by clicking on the “SAP HANA Connection Management” indicator in the bottom right corner and choosing “SAP HANA SQL” from the subsequent dialog.

Execute SQL Queries

Add one or more SQL statements and execute the code block. The results will be shown below the code block.

Add SQL Statements and Execute the Code Block

You can add more code and markdown blocks as needed.

 

Use the Joule functionality to enhance your MDK projects in SAP Build Code

Remember back in June when we announced you could create and manage your mobile development kit (MDK) apps directly from the SAP Build lobby and continue their development using the Storyboard in SAP Build Code? We're thrilled to share another exciting update on that front!

You can now take advantage of the generative AI assistant Joule to enhance your existing MDK projects in SAP Build Code. For further details, I recommend to read this blogpost written by my colleague Jitendra.

 

Devtoberfest and TechEd

Get ready for an exciting journey of learning and collaboration with Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd Virtual!

From September 23rd to October 25th, 2024, join us for Devtoberfest, a celebration of coding and collaboration within the SAP Community. This virtual event is designed to prepare you for the highly anticipated SAP TechEd. My colleague Ravi summarizes all you need to know about SAP Build Code at Devtoberfest. For further information, read the Devtoberfest 2024 Welcome blog post and join the Devtoberfest group in SAP Community to take part in this great event!

And don't forget to mark your calendar for SAP TechEd Virtual on October 8-9, 2024, your gateway to the latest innovations and advancements in SAP technologies! The session catalog is now live, featuring a wide range of topics across various tracks, including the Application Development and Automation and the Data and Analytics tracks, where you'll find dedicated sessions on SAP Build Code. You are looking for a summary where to find SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd? Then I recommend to read this blog post.

Don't miss these opportunities to enhance your technical expertise and connect with the SAP community.

 

Let Us Hear Your Voice!

We prepared a short survey, focused on improving the product to better fit your needs. By participating, you can help us improve SAP Build Code and SAP Business Application Studio. It should only take 5 minutes to complete. Please take the survey now.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: SAP Business AI Testing = SAP API Testing
entry.content:
Introduction - why testing APIs is important for SAP Business AI 

When we talk about SAP Business AI in business we need to remember one thing: AI cannot work without APIs. In fact, testing SAP Business AI is all about testing how well APIs work. Let’s break down why this is so important.

Figure 1 - generative AI cannot do any business activity without SAP APIs 

Only AI powered Chatbots in SAP Business AI? 

SAP Business AI has a potential to eliminate all frontend applications (SAP GUI, Fiori and all other front end applications) and leave only chatbots (Joule, Copilot, etc.) to work with all SAP business functions of SAP S/4HANA. This might mean that all business functions will be exposed via APIs. 

Figure 2 - SAP Gui - sailing away into the past 

Why APIs Are Important for SAP Business AI

APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) are like bridges that let different programs talk to each other. AI, especially advanced AI like generative AI, needs APIs to get information and connect with other systems. Without these connections, AI can't do much.

Integration is the backbone of digital transformations, and APIs are the building blocks for every SAP customer who sells, buys, or transports their products. This means APIs are the key to making all of a company’s systems work together. For AI, APIs are what allow it to be part of that big picture.

How APIs Help SAP Business AI Work

Think of AI like a chef who needs ingredients to make a meal. The API is the helper that brings those ingredients to the kitchen. Without it, the chef (AI) can’t do their job. Here’s how APIs help AI:

  1. Getting Data: AI needs a lot of information to make smart decisions. APIs bring that data from different places to the AI.
  2. Executing Business Activities: To do any business task—like creating an order, delivering goods, or managing inventory—AI needs to use APIs to make things happen. For example, if AI is asked to create a new sales order, it has to send a request through an API to the system that manages orders. Without APIs, AI wouldn’t be able to carry out any business activities.
  3. Building New Solutions: APIs allow businesses to create new smart tools by connecting AI with existing software, kind of like mixing different ingredients to create a new recipe.

Figure 3 - SAP API Testing with Int4 Suite = SAP Business AI testing 

How does SAP Business AI execute business activities on SAP S/4HANA

SAP has many ways to expose it’s SAP S/4HANA and their use depends on the context:

  1. SAP APIs (Webservices, ODATA, etc.) can be exposed directly from SAP S/4HANA. Some typical ways include generating web services or ODATA interfaces.
  2. In order to manage APIs exposed by SAP S/4HANA some other components might be used like SAP BTP API Management (part of SAP Integration Suite) and those APIs can also be exposed by SAP Business Accelerator Hub – https://api.sap.com
  3. In most cases however SAP APIs are build and designed using SAP Integration Suite. SAP Integration Suite is the go to solution for building the SAP S/4HANA Clean Core side by side extensions which include building and orchestrating complex integration scenarios and exposing them to the outside world (to SAP Business AI, Joule, Copilots, etc.)

Why Testing SAP Business AI = Testing SAP APIs

Since APIs are so important to AI, testing SAP Business AI is really about testing how well the APIs work. If the APIs don’t work right, the AI won’t either. Here are some key things to check when testing APIs:

  1. Getting and Posting the Right Data: Making sure the API sends the right information to the AI, so it can make the best decisions and making sure that APIs exposed to AI are working as they we designed
  2. Working Together: Testing if AI and other SAP systems can communicate smoothly through APIs.
  3. Speed and Performance: Ensuring the APIs work quickly and don’t slow down the AI’s responses.

In short, AI can’t exist without APIs. And in SAP systems, this connection is even more important because everything is linked together through APIs. So, if you're testing SAP Business AI, you're really testing the APIs that make it all possible.

Further Reference

Learn how to test SAP APIs with Int4 Suite - Avoid SAP S/4HANA Project Delays with Third-Party Systems Service Virtualization

entry.title: What’s new with Joule in the upcoming SAP SuccessFactors 2H 2024 release
entry.content:
Joule recently celebrated its first birthday and it’s amazing how much it has grown since being introduced just one year ago. Over the last 12 months, SAP has continuously enhanced Joule’s copilot capabilities with innovations that improve the conversational experience and increase the value the solution can bring to our customers. Many of the enhancements delivered so far and many more on the solution roadmap are the result of some specific end-user expectations of what a copilot should deliver:

  1. People expect Joule to deliver a more “ChatGPT-like” experience
  2. People expect a smoother conversational experience from a copilot
  3. People are eager to talk to Joule in additional languages beyond English

Let’s review some of the ways Joule is growing to meet these expectations from our SAP SuccessFactors community in our upcoming release.

Enhanced conversational experience and new Joule use cases

In this release, we are introducing Joule Functions, a new conversational experience that enables smarter, more adaptive conversations by managing dialog flows, improving language understanding, and keeping track of chat history and user context for seamless interactions. What can users expect from this new conversational experience?

  • Joule users will enjoy more natural, human-like conversations with more accurate intent recognition.
  • Users can now more easily switch between topics while keeping chat history and context intact.
  • Joule will provide more precise answers tailored to your previous interactions and context.

For example, as shown in the images below, if a user asks to generate feedback to send to an employee, Joule will retain the context of the conversation, allowing the user to request additional updates to the generated content before it is sent. Rather than following predefined paths, Joule responds even more intelligently to the user’s needs, offering a fluid and natural conversational experience.

 

 Figure 1 – An example of Joule’s new enhanced conversational experience

The following new transactional use cases are included in this release and take advantage of the new conversational experience:

  • AI-assisted onboarding task completion - Helps improve the efficiency of HR Partners and Managers by completing onboarding tasks via Joule.
  • View performance and development goals - Employees can now efficiently access a list of performance and development goals, as well as review goal details, by using conversational chat in Joule.

 Figure 2 – An example of AI-assisted onboarding task completion via Joule

Additional new navigational use cases that take advantage of the new conversational experience are included in this release, including the ability to assist new hires during onboarding as well as the ability to view information from Opportunity Marketplace, Growth Portfolio, and job requisitions.

All of the aforementioned new Joule use cases will be available with the production release on November 15, 2024. After that date, we will resume delivering new Joule features and use cases on a monthly basis and we will also continue to issue the associated What’s New Viewer (WNV) documents each month. It’s important to note that the WNV will not reflect these new items until November 15.

A total of 70 existing Joule use cases are being upgraded to take advantage of the new conversational experience. It's important to note that the HR Policy FAQ use case has already been upgraded for the new conversational experience and is available for use today. Please see the Appendix in this blog for a complete list of the Joule use cases that are being upgraded in conjunction with the 2H 2024 release. All use cases (whether transactional or navigational) not upgraded in this release will be upgraded in the monthly Joule releases beginning in December 2024.

New Joule data center strategy

To make it easier for customers to adopt Joule, we have enhanced the Joule data center consumption strategy. This change introduces flexibility by allowing customers to determine which Joule data center they wish to use. In addition, this change supports global customers or those with complex landscapes who choose to integrate Joule with multiple SAP business applications on different data centers, even across different regions. SAP will continue to introduce new Joule data centers in other regions and will always consider unique customer migration needs.

Please refer to the Joule product document Data Centers Supported by Joule to understand the Joule data center details as well as the instruction on how to determine a Joule data center.

An update on Joule language availability

Four critical languages – German, Spanish, French, and Portuguese – will be supported in Joule beginning in early 2025. Several other additional languages like Japanese, Korean, Dutch, and Chinese are planned to follow in the Joule roadmap (delivery timelines not available as of this writing).

Summary

As mentioned above, it is important to remember that all new Joule features and use cases will be available to customers beginning with the 2H production release on November 15, 2024. This release brings an innovative new conversational experience to Joule, as well as several new Joule use cases, a new Joule data center strategy, and updates to the ongoing language rollout for Joule.

Finally, some SAP SuccessFactors Joule content such as access to profile, feedback, approvals, and HR policy questions will also be accessible via the SAP Mobile Start app beginning in late November, 2024. More details on that to come!

Appendix

List of 70 Joule use cases upgraded to the new conversational experience in 2H 2024 release:

  • Compensation  |  View award balance
  • Compensation  |  View compensation forms
  • Employee Central  |  View addresses
  • Employee Central  |  View email addresses

  • Performance & Goals  |  View 360 reviews forms

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 - Summary
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 delivered an inspiring keynote with a strong focus on how technology drives real-world business innovations. This session brought to light the latest advances in AI, cloud solutions, and SAP's vision for the future of enterprise software.

The keynote opened with a thought-provoking introduction about the human brain, its complexities, and how innovation emerges from millions of connections forming new ideas. This metaphor seamlessly tied into SAP's philosophy of turning ideas into tangible solutions—where concepts become tools for solving real business problems.

Innovation at the Heart of SAP’s Strategy

One of the central themes was the growing importance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP’s offerings. AI is now deeply integrated into SAP’s cloud-driven portfolio, designed to simplify customers' lives and meet evolving business needs. Senior AI Scientists at SAP are working on harnessing AI to make business software smarter and more intuitive. The keynote also emphasized SAP's efforts to ensure that its software is configurable, extensible, and unified, providing a seamless suite-like experience across its platforms.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

A significant highlight was SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP), which has seen rapid growth in customer adoption. BTP is the cornerstone for SAP’s cloud and AI-driven future, empowering businesses with application development, automation, data analytics, and AI capabilities all within one unified platform. The keynote highlighted that over 27,000 live customers and 2,800 partners are leveraging SAP BTP to drive innovation.

The announcement of new BTP regions running on infrastructure from partners like AWS, Microsoft, and Google, along with SAP-managed data centers, shows SAP’s commitment to providing broader regional coverage and reliable service. This expansion aims to help businesses operate globally while maintaining efficiency, security, and compliance.

Enhancements in Application Development and Automation

SAP unveiled its Clean Core Strategy, which allows businesses to build custom extensions in a way that keeps the core ERP system maintainable and upgradeable. With SAP’s low-code environment, developers can create AI-powered applications with ease, while ABAP Cloud now supports both on-stack and side-by-side development, giving businesses more flexibility in their customizations.

One exciting feature introduced was the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, a tool designed to streamline the process of creating custom fields and extensions, allowing users to enhance SAP applications with minimal coding.

SAP Build and GenAI

In keeping with the AI theme, SAP showcased the GenAI capabilities within SAP Build. With Joule, an AI assistant, developers can create complex data models, build extensions, and generate applications through natural language prompts. This AI-driven approach reduces manual coding and helps users integrate advanced functionalities like time series forecasting and predictive analytics into their business processes.

Data and Analytics Innovations

The keynote further highlighted innovations in data and analytics, specifically focusing on SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Datasphere. The HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine were introduced, both of which enable businesses to harness structured and unstructured data for more context-aware AI-driven decisions. These engines promise faster and more accurate data processing, particularly in scenarios involving large language models and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

Additionally, the keynote showcased how SAP Datasphere is being adopted by large enterprises like Hershey’s and Bosch for data cataloging, integration, and self-service analytics, empowering businesses to access trusted data with ease.

Integration Suite and AI-Driven Optimization

The SAP Integration Suite now offers a modern, AI-powered solution for real-time end-to-end business process integration. With thousands of pre-built integrations and connectors, businesses can seamlessly integrate SAP and third-party applications. SAP is also leveraging AI-generated recommendations to optimize custom scripts, further enhancing developer productivity and reducing memory consumption.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 demonstrated SAP’s unwavering commitment to making innovation accessible, impactful, and transformative. From the integration of advanced AI capabilities to the expansion of BTP, SAP is setting a new standard in enterprise software. Whether you are looking to enhance your application development, automate processes, or harness the power of data, SAP’s latest innovations offer powerful tools to bring your ideas to life.

As SAP continues to evolve, the future of enterprise technology is more connected, intelligent, and adaptable than ever before.

Here is the Keynote from SAP:

Comment below your thoughts on TechEd.

entry.title: SAP User Experience News for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Q4/2023
entry.content:
Joule is now generally available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023. Joule saves you a lot of time when you need to refer to documentation, by giving you a concise summary and pointing you to relevant sections. It also helps you find the right application for well over one hundred navigational scenarios, as well as providing some transactional interactions for selected business objects. If you move up to Feature Pack Stack 2 (FPS2), you can also benefit from built-in collaboration, with the SAP Collaboration Manager now generally available with enhanced functionality. Talking about collaboration, FPS2 also enables users to initiate a chat or call directly from a contact card via Microsoft Teams integration.

This post covers:

  • Joule
  • New collaboration capabilities via:
    -  SAP Collaboration Manager
    -  Contact card integration with Microsoft Teams
  • SAP Mobile Start update
  • News for UI developers, web and mobile

If you want to see how good SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition now looks, across all the main product areas, then I thoroughly recommend that you have a look at the new online Product Tour.

In case you haven’t already seen these, do have a look at the user experience innovations which came with FPS00 and FPS01:

  • SAP User Experience Update: What’s New for SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)
  • User Experience Advances with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)

Joule

Users can save around ten minutes every time they need to refer to the documentation, by using Joule to give them a concise summary, created using generative AI. The response also provides three links to the most relevant sections in the documentation. We call this the Informational Pattern. Figure 1 below shows an example for someone working in sales, and Figure 2 shows an example for someone working in procurement.

Figure 1: Using Joule in sales to get help from the documentation, in this case asking “How can I make mass changes to sales orders?”. Alt Text: An image showing Joule on the left, with the query text and an eleven line response below it, followed by the option to expand to see the search results. On the right, an image of Joule is shown with the search results expanded to show three results, the first one called “Mass Changes of Sales Documents”, referring to the documentation of the app with that name. Each search result has a heading with three lines of explanatory text below it.

 

 

 

Figure 2: Using Joule in procurement to get help from the documentation, in this case asking “Can I configure a workflow for my purchase requisitions?”. Alt Text: An image showing Joule on the left, with the query text and a nine line response below it, followed by the option to expand to see the search results. On the right, an image of Joule is shown with the search results expanded to show three results, the first one called “How to Configure the Flexible Workflow for Purc…”, referring to the documentation. Each search result has a heading with three lines of explanatory text below it.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023, users can also benefit from the navigational pattern for over one hundred use cases, in areas such as procurement, finance, sales, and service management. This pattern allows them to navigate directly to apps relevant to their query. You can find out more in this blog post:

  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

 Furthermore, we provide some selected transactional use cases in these areas too.

 To find out more about Joule, have a look at the documentation:

  • Joule in SAP Help Portal

Planned Innovations

As announced at SAP TechEd on October 8th, we have many plans for making Joule even more useful for users, as well as enabling you to build your own skills to extend what SAP delivers. As always, our plans are subject to change, but of course we hope to deliver as planned.

Some highlights are:

  • Joule for Mobile, built into SAP Mobile Start. As announced, this is planned to become available in November 2024.
  • Joule studio in SAP Build for building your own skills is planned to become available for beta testing later this year, and become generally available in Q1/2025. Get a preview by watching the recording of this TechEd talk:
    -  AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Beyond that, we are working on having many Joule agents interact with each other, across our products – making Joule agentic. Also, to support hands-free interaction, we are working on integration with Siri, so that users can use voice to interact.

 If you haven’t watched it live, then have a look at the replay of the TechEd talk by our AI leaders:

  • AI100 | Artificial intelligence that creates real-world results

New Collaboration Capabilities

With Feature Pack Stack 2 we introduce the following new collaboration capabilities.

SAP Collaboration Manager

SAP Collaboration Manager allows users to:

  • Take notes when using an application; these notes are pinned to the application, and are listed in the Relevant Chats tab when a user has that application open in the same window as the SAP Collaboration Manager.
  • Ad-hoc chat with colleagues, directly in the SAP system.
  • Share and annotate screenshots; a link is provided to the recipient with which they can navigate directly to the UI where the screenshot was taken.
  • Share the business context.
  • Upload and share files.

Figure 3 shows an example where a user Jones Davidson has created a chat with a colleague Alyssa Arnold and shared a screenshot along with a request to her to take a look at the two open sales orders in the screenshot.

For a closer look at some of the above features, watch it in action here in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editions 2308 system (what is shown is now available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02):

  • Video: SAP Collaboration Manager (2:10 minutes)

Figure 3: Using SAP Collaboration Manager to chat with a colleague and share a screenshot Alt Text: An image showing a list of three sales orders selected in the Manage Sales Orders app, and SAP Collaboration Manager as an overlay on the right showing the chat text and the screenshot image.

Beyond the above features, users can now also:

  • Mark their favorites, and filter by them (using the Favorites tab).
  • Set chats to unread.
  • Add and share insight cards from My Home.

These features are shown in Figure 4.

Figure 4: On the left: SAP Collaboration Manager offers tabs for “Recent chats”, “All Chats” and “Favorites”. You see the star indicating a favorite; the blue bar on the left of the second chat indicates that it has been set to unread. On the right: an insight card added to a chat. Alt Text: An image showing two screenshots of SAP Collaboration Manager. On the left, showing “All Chats” tab with a list of three chats. The second entry in the list is marked as unread, and has a star indicating it is a favorite. On the right, showing an insight card with a vertical bar chart embedded in the chat.

Contact card integration with Microsoft Teams

Users can now initiate a chat or call with Microsoft Teams directly from a contact card in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02. This feature is also available for SAP S/4HANA 2023 (on-premise) with FPS02.

Figure 5 shows two examples for this: using it directly on the My Home page to contact the creator of a task, and using it to contact a customer directly out of a sales order.

Figure 5: Two examples for how you can initiate a chat or call from a contact card via Microsoft Teams – from a To-Do card in My Home (upper image) and from a sold-to party link (lower image). Alt Text: An image showing two screenshots of popovers next to links referring to contacts -  for the ToDo card the contact is the creator of the task, for the other one it is the sold-to-party of the sales order. Each popover contains a section for initiating a Microsoft Teams collaboration with icons for chat, audio call and video call.

SAP Mobile Start

Users like to feel in charge, and a good way to support that is to let them define their favorites. The My Home page provides this feature and, as we saw above, the SAP Collaboration Manager has introduced this capability too.

Now also SAP Mobile Start lets users very easily define up to six favorites, two of which appear on the start screen next to a “See all” link to show all six. Figure 6 shows you what this looks like.

Figure 6: SAP Mobile Start lets users define their favorite apps / tiles. Alt Text: Three screenshots of SAP Mobile Start are shown. The one on the left shows the start page, which now has a “Favorites” section at the top, above the “Latest To-Dos” and “Insights” sections. The Favorites section shows two apps, each with a KPI shown as a number; on the right it has a “See all” link. The image in the middle shows all the favorites, in this case three are listed. The image on the right shows a list of all apps which can be selected as a favorite.

Find out more about this, as well as the new capability to upload and download attachments from tasks in this blog post:

  • SAP Mobile Start V1.12 - Release Update

News for UI Developers, Web and Mobile

The major news for UI developers for both web and mobile is that SAP Build now supports ABAP Cloud, and not just CAP (Cloud Application Programming model) for other programming languages such as Java and JavaScript. Get a comprehensive overview in this blog post from Michael Ameling:

  • Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud!

Providing Joule skills is also a powerful way to enhance the user experience – I covered our main planned innovations there in already in the “Planned Innovations” part of the above Joule section.

News for Web Developers

Alt Text: Image of the white UI5 logo on a blue background with the text “UI5 linter 1.0 New Release” below it.

The main news is that UI5 linter 1.0 has now been released and is generally available. It is a static code analysis tool which helps ensure that you have good quality code, and which saves you a lot of time debugging later by catching issues up front. It can check for problems such as the use of deprecated UI5 libraries, framework APIs etc. as well as the usage of global variables and possible CSP violations. Find out more in this blot post:

  • Release of UI5 linter v1.0

I would also like to point you to the updated SAP BTP Developer’s Guide, where we have now brought in user experience design advice, as well as restructuring it to cover Explore, Discover, Design, Deliver and Scale & Run phases:

  • SAP BTP Developer’s Guide
  • If you read the guide, do spend a few minutes to give us feedback, so that we can improve it even further, by taking this survey to help us improve the guide!

The developer’s guide recommends using SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements, built on OData services, for developing UIs. Should you not be able to follow this recommendation because you have existing applications using different protocols or frameworks, we have options to help you nevertheless:

  • If you have existing back-end code which cannot provide OData services, we have now released the SAPUI5 meta-data controls library sap.ui.mdc, as outlined here:
    -  Smart choices for your UI: When to use sap.ui.mdc
  • If you already have applications written with a different UI technology, such as Angular or React, we have released Version 2.0 of our UI5 Web Components:
    -  Blast Off! UI5 Web Components 2.0 is Now Live!

For friends of ABAP, we recently introduced the ability to create Fiori elements apps directly from within the ABAP Development Tools (ADT). Find out more here:

  • How to create a SAP Fiori Elements application from within ADT?
  • SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release adds closer integration with ABAP Development Tools

News for Mobile Developers

The main news for mobile developers is the latest releases of the mobile SDKs, as well as the integration of the Mobile Development Kit into SAP Build Code. Here are links to find out more:

  • How to Develop SAP MDK Applications in SAP Build Code
  • Streamline SAP MDK Development with the Power of Generative AI and Joule
  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS 24.8.0 is now available
  • SAP BTP SDK for Android 24.8.0 is now available

For a deeper dive, watch the recordings of the Devtoberfest sessions on developing mobile apps:

  • Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK (50:40 minutes)
  • Craft Enterprise Apps for iOS, iPadOS, and visionOS with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code (47:16 minutes)
  • Build Elegant Enterprise Apps for Android with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code (45:24 minutes)

Final Words

I hope you are as excited about these new user experience innovations as I am! FPS02 brings quite a few additional new features beyond user experience, discover all of them here:

  • What’s New Viewer for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02

If you like what you see here, we encourage you to upgrade to FPS02, so that you can run your business even better with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023.
